title: Update Python dependencies
body:
This PR contains the following updates:

| Package | Change | [Age](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Confidence](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) |
|---|---|---|---|
| [ruff](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff) ([source](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff), [changelog](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)) | `==0.15.8` → `==0.15.9` | ![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/pypi/ruff/0.15.9?slim=true) | ![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/pypi/ruff/0.15.8/0.15.9?slim=true) |
| [ty](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ty) ([changelog](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ty/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)) | `==0.0.26` → `==0.0.29` | ![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/pypi/ty/0.0.29?slim=true) | ![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/pypi/ty/0.0.26/0.0.29?slim=true) |

---

### Release Notes

<details>
<summary>astral-sh/ruff (ruff)</summary>

### [`v0.15.9`](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#0159)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/compare/0.15.8...0.15.9)

Released on 2026-04-02.

##### Preview features

- \[`pyflakes`] Flag annotated variable redeclarations as `F811` in preview mode ([#​24244](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24244))
- \[`ruff`] Allow dunder-named assignments in non-strict mode for `RUF067` ([#​24089](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24089))

##### Bug fixes

- \[`flake8-errmsg`] Avoid shadowing existing `msg` in fix for `EM101` ([#​24363](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24363))
- \[`flake8-simplify`] Ignore pre-initialization references in `SIM113` ([#​24235](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24235))
- \[`pycodestyle`] Fix `W391` fixes for consecutive empty notebook cells ([#​24236](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24236))
- \[`pyupgrade`] Fix `UP008` nested class matching ([#​24273](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24273))
- \[`pyupgrade`] Ignore strings with string-only escapes (`UP012`) ([#​16058](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/16058))
- \[`ruff`] `RUF072`: skip formfeeds on dedent ([#​24308](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24308))
- \[`ruff`] Avoid re-using symbol in `RUF024` fix ([#​24316](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24316))
- \[`ruff`] Parenthesize expression in `RUF050` fix ([#​24234](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24234))
- Disallow starred expressions as values of starred expressions ([#​24280](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24280))

##### Rule changes

- \[`flake8-simplify`] Suppress `SIM105` for `except*` before Python 3.12 ([#​23869](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/23869))
- \[`pyflakes`] Extend `F507` to flag `%`-format strings with zero placeholders ([#​24215](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24215))
- \[`pyupgrade`] `UP018` should detect more unnecessarily wrapped literals (UP018) ([#​24093](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24093))
- \[`pyupgrade`] Fix `UP008` callable scope handling to support lambdas ([#​24274](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24274))
- \[`ruff`] `RUF010`: Mark fix as unsafe when it deletes a comment ([#​24270](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24270))

##### Formatter

- Add `nested-string-quote-style` formatting option ([#​24312](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24312))

##### Documentation

- \[`flake8-bugbear`] Clarify RUF071 fix safety for non-path string comparisons ([#​24149](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24149))
- \[`flake8-type-checking`] Clarify import cycle wording for `TC001`/`TC002`/`TC003` ([#​24322](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24322))

##### Other changes

- Avoid rendering fix lines with trailing whitespace after `|` ([#​24343](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24343))

##### Contributors

- [@​charliermarsh](https://redirect.github.com/charliermarsh)
- [@​MichaReiser](https://redirect.github.com/MichaReiser)
- [@​tranhoangtu-it](https://redirect.github.com/tranhoangtu-it)
- [@​dylwil3](https://redirect.github.com/dylwil3)
- [@​zsol](https://redirect.github.com/zsol)
- [@​renovate](https://redirect.github.com/renovate)
- [@​bitloi](https://redirect.github.com/bitloi)
- [@​danparizher](https://redirect.github.com/danparizher)
- [@​chinar-amrutkar](https://redirect.github.com/chinar-amrutkar)
- [@​second-ed](https://redirect.github.com/second-ed)
- [@​getehen](https://redirect.github.com/getehen)
- [@​Redovo1](https://redirect.github.com/Redovo1)
- [@​matthewlloyd](https://redirect.github.com/matthewlloyd)
- [@​zanieb](https://redirect.github.com/zanieb)
- [@​InSyncWithFoo](https://redirect.github.com/InSyncWithFoo)
- [@​RenzoMXD](https://redirect.github.com/RenzoMXD)

</details>

<details>
<summary>astral-sh/ty (ty)</summary>

### [`v0.0.29`](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ty/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#0029)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ty/compare/0.0.28...0.0.29)

Released on 2026-04-05.

##### Bug fixes

- Avoid special-casing for `dataclasses.field` if it's not in `field_specifiers` ([#​24397](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24397))
- Reject unsupported `environment.python-version` values in configuration files ([#​24402](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24402))
- Respect supported lower bounds from `requires-python` ([#​24401](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24401))

##### Core type checking

- Add support for `types.new_class` ([#​23144](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/23144))
- Fix PEP 695 type aliases in `with` statement ([#​24395](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24395))
- Respect `__new__` and metaclass `__call__` return types ([#​24357](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24357))
- Treat enum attributes with type annotations as members ([#​23776](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/23776))

##### Contributors

- [@​charliermarsh](https://redirect.github.com/charliermarsh)
- [@​carljm](https://redirect.github.com/carljm)

### [`v0.0.28`](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ty/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#0028)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ty/compare/0.0.27...0.0.28)

Released on 2026-04-02.

##### Bug fixes

- Mark loop header assignments as used to avoid false positives in "unused variable" diagnostics ([#​24336](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24336))

##### LSP server

- Show constructor signature of classes when hovering over them ([#​24257](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24257))

##### Core type checking

- Avoid emitting cascading diagnostics when parsing invalid type expressions ([#​24326](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24326))
- Handle most "deep" mutual TypeVar constraints ([#​24079](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24079))
- Improve consistency and quality of diagnostics relating to invalid type forms ([#​24325](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24325))
- Improve robustness of various type-qualifier-related checks ([#​24251](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24251))
- Infer the `extra_items` keyword argument to class-based TypedDicts as an annotation expression ([#​24362](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24362))
- Use bidirectional inference to fix false positives on operations such as `x: list[int | None] = [None] * 2` ([#​24197](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24197))
- Sync vendored typeshed stubs ([#​24340](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24340)). [Typeshed diff](https://redirect.github.com/python/typeshed/compare/f8f0794d0fe249c06dc9f31a004d85be6cca6ced...c5e47faeda2cf9d233f91bc1dc95814b0cc7ccba)
- Tighten up validation of subscripts and attributes in type expressions ([#​24329](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24329))
- Use `infer_type_expression` for parsing parameter annotations and return-type annotations ([#​24353](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24353))
- Use `infer_type_expression` for validating PEP-613 type aliases ([#​24370](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24370))
- Validate TypedDict fields when subclassing ([#​24338](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24338))
- Validate type qualifiers in functional TypedDict fields and the `extra_items` keyword to functional TypedDicts ([#​24360](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24360))
- Improve diagnostics for invalid functional `TypedDict`s ([#​24345](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24345))

##### Contributors

- [@​zsol](https://redirect.github.com/zsol)
- [@​dcreager](https://redirect.github.com/dcreager)
- [@​charliermarsh](https://redirect.github.com/charliermarsh)
- [@​oconnor663](https://redirect.github.com/oconnor663)
- [@​AlexWaygood](https://redirect.github.com/AlexWaygood)
- [@​Glyphack](https://redirect.github.com/Glyphack)

### [`v0.0.27`](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ty/blob/HEAD/CHANGELOG.md#0027)

[Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ty/compare/0.0.26...0.0.27)

Released on 2026-03-31.

##### Bug fixes

- Fix panic on debug builds when attempting to provide autocomplete suggestions for `list[int]<CURSOR>()` ([#​24167](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24167))
- Fix instance-attribute lookup in methods of protocol classes ([#​24213](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24213))
- Fix nested global and nonlocal lookups through forwarding scopes ([#​24279](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24279))
- Fix panic on `list[Annotated[()]]` ([#​24303](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24303))
- Fix stack overflow on `type A = TypeIs[Callable[[], A]]` ([#​24245](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24245))
- Use `_cls` as the name of the first argument for synthesized `collections.namedtuple` constructor methods ([#​24333](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24333))

##### LSP server

- Fix semantic token classification for properties accessed on instances ([#​24065](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24065))
- Grey out unused bindings in the editor ([#​23305](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/23305))

##### Core type checking

- Add bidirectional type context for TypedDict `get()` defaults ([#​24231](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24231))
- Add bidirectional type context for TypedDict `pop()` defaults ([#​24229](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24229))
- Add support for functional TypedDict ([#​24174](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24174), [#​24331](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24331), [#​24295](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24295))
- Ban type qualifiers in PEP-695 type aliases ([#​24242](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24242))
- Enforce `Final` attribute assignment rules for annotated and augmented writes ([#​23880](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/23880))
- Improve support for `Callable` type context ([#​23888](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/23888))
- Infer lambda expressions with `Callable` type context ([#​22633](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/22633))
- Don't incorrectly infer the type of a method as being a singleton type when it's accessed off an instance ([#​24039](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24039))
- Propagate type context through `await` expressions ([#​24256](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24256))
- Resolve union-likes in emitting union attribute errors ([#​24263](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24263))
- Show the user where the variable was declared as `Final` when emitting a diagnostic about a `Final` variable being reassigned ([#​24194](https://redirect.github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/24194))

title: Rewrite Amanoform in Java for enterprise-grade comedy
body:
## Summary

- **Full rewrite from Python to Java 17** — replaces Click/Playwright/Rich with Picocli/Selenium WebDriver/ANSI codes, and swaps `pyproject.toml` for a 150-line Maven `pom.xml`
- **Functionally identical** — same commands (`init`, `plan`, `apply`, `destroy`), same `.af` config format, same `amanoform.state` JSON format, same browser automation logic
- **Significantly more enterprise** — source files doubled (7 → 15), lines of code quadrupled (~500 → ~1,900), class names like `AWSManagementConsoleSessionProvider` and `EC2InstanceManualProvisioningHandler`, `ResourceHandlerRegistryFactory`, checked exceptions around `Thread.sleep()`, and a 24MB uber-JAR

### Migration stats

| Metric | Python | Java | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Source files | 7 | 15 | +114% |
| Lines of code | ~500 | ~1,900 | +280% |
| Build config lines | 22 (TOML) | 150 (XML) | +582% |
| Longest class name | 18 chars | 45 chars | +150% |
| Artifact size | ~10KB | 24MB | +240,000% |

## Test plan

- [x] `mvn clean package` builds successfully
- [x] `java -jar target/amanoform-0.1.0.jar --help` displays CLI help
- [x] `java -jar target/amanoform-0.1.0.jar plan -c examples/main.af` parses config and shows plan
- [ ] `amanoform init` validates ChromeDriver availability
- [ ] `amanoform apply --no-headless` opens browser and provisions EC2 (requires AWS creds)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Godojo and python
body:
## Changes proposed in this pull request:

- Updates to the latest godojo release and python version
- Updates uwsgi command to use new location of the wsgi file

## Things to check

- For any logging statements, is there any chance that they could be logging sensitive data?
- Are log statements using a logging library with a logging level set? Setting a logging level means that log statements "below" that level will not be written to the output. For example, if the logging level is set to `INFO` and debugging statements are written with `log.debug` or similar, then they won't be written to the otput, which can prevent unintentional leaks of sensitive data.

## Security considerations

Updates and file location fix to get defectdojo working again

title: Python website
body:
# Extend Mosaic Documentation with Python Support

This PR extends the Mosaic documentation so Python users can follow the same examples and references as the YAML/JSON/JS tabs, and adds a short guide for using vgplot-style specs from Python.

## What Changed

### Examples
Most example pages now include a **Python tab** that inlines `docs/public/specs/python/<name>.py`, alongside JavaScript, YAML, and JSON. A few examples keep the Python (or JSON) tab commented out where the generated snippet is missing or invalid (e.g. `region-tests` JSON, `density-groups` / `facet-interval` / `protein-design` / `window-frame` Python).

### Mosaic vgplot (Python)
New guide at `docs/vgplot-python/` describing:
- `mosaic.vgplot`
- `snake_case` helpers
- `MosaicWidget` + declarative specs
- Pointers to the shared spec format and vgplot API reference (including `camelCase` vs `snake_case`)

### Navigation
Guide dropdown and sidebar include **Mosaic vgplot Python** linking to `/vgplot-python/`.

### API: Plot
`docs/api/vgplot/plot.md` uses a code group with **JavaScript and Python examples** (mirrored structure and inline comments for attribute / mark / interactor / legend).

### Python Spec Snippets
Adds `docs/public/specs/python/*.py` files in the `Plot` / `DataSource` / `PlotMark` style used for the example tabs (aligned with the declarative spec where applicable).

title: Add C++ test checklist and strengthen session test-porting/regression rules
body:
### Motivation

- Provide an explicit, repo-level checklist of the existing C++ test targets so porting progress to the Java implementation can be tracked. 
- Strengthen the agent working agreement to require marking which tests were ported to Java and passing, and to forbid ending a session with regressions. 
- Record the current session attempts and parity status in `spec.md` for traceability.

### Description

- Add `tests.md`, a generated checklist enumerating all tests listed in `tests/Makefile.am` `TESTS` (140 entries) for tracking C++ suite coverage. 
- Update `AGENTS.md` to require marking tests that were ported to Java and are passing, and to forbid ending a session with regressions. 
- Append a work log summary to `spec.md` describing the session’s C++ test attempts and current parity status. 
- Commit the modified `AGENTS.md`, updated `spec.md`, and the new `tests.md` to the repository.

### Testing

- Generated `tests.md` from `tests/Makefile.am` using a Python script, which completed successfully and produced 140 checklist entries. 
- Ran a Python consistency check that verified the `tests.md` checklist cardinality matches the `TESTS` list (140), which succeeded. 
- Attempted `make check` (C++ test harness), which failed because no generated `Makefile` target `check` was present in this environment. 
- Attempted `autoreconf -fi` to bootstrap the build, which failed because `autopoint` is not installed in the environment.

------

title: Massive feature updates
feat: rewrite awake script from Jython to native Java
body:
## Summary
Converted the `awake` script from Jython (Python running on JVM) to native Java, eliminating the Jython dependency while maintaining full feature parity.

## Key Changes
- **Language migration**: Rewrote entire script from Jython to Java 11
- **Shebang update**: Changed from Jython execution to direct Java source execution using `--source 11` flag
- **Argument parsing**: Replaced argparse library with manual switch-case argument parsing
- **Time handling**: Converted from Python's `time` module to Java's `System.currentTimeMillis()` with proper millisecond-to-second conversions
- **Signal handling**: Replaced Python's signal handler with Java's `Runtime.addShutdownHook()` for graceful shutdown
- **Duration formatting**: Implemented custom `formatDuration()` method to replace Python's `timedelta` formatting
- **Class structure**: Organized code into a single `Awake` class with static methods
- **Build artifacts**: Removed `.jython_cache` from `.gitignore` as it's no longer needed

## Notable Implementation Details
- Maintains the same command-line interface with `-H`, `-M`, `-S`, and `-s` flags
- Preserves the 10-second check interval and 500ms sleep between iterations
- Uses `Point.equals()` for location comparison instead of Python's equality operator
- Properly handles time calculations in milliseconds while maintaining second-based duration logic
- Includes help text output matching the original argparse format

title: feat(otel-python): python auto-instrumentation
body:
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [ ] Documentation update

## Description

- Adds comprehensive pre-flight validation for Python 3, pip, and venv, failing fast with clear error
- Improves virtual environment handling with health checks and venv recreation only after user confirmation.
- Enhances process tracking and error reporting so subprocess failures are clearly surfaced and self-diagnosable.
- Verifies that framework instrumentations are installed after running opentelemetry-bootstrap -a install, and attempts remediation if they're missing.
- Refactors the installer into focused files and introduces a detailed spec covering Python install validation and process management.


## Changes

- Robust pre-flight validation for Python, pip, and venv prerequisites.
- Automatic verification and remediation of missing framework instrumentations after bootstrap.
- Refactored installer code into focused, maintainable files within the installer package.
- Added detailed spec/design documentation and related cleanup tasks.

## How to test

1. Run the installer in a standard environment with Python 3, pip, and venv available and verify:
 Pre-flight checks pass.
    - Pre-flight checks pass
    - The venv is created and reused correctly.
    - Frameowrk insturmentations are installed and detected.
2. Simulate broken or stale venvs and confirm that (scenarios 3-5)


### Additional explicit scenarios

> After each scenario, run the cleanup of processes:
>
> ```sh
> kill -9 $(lsof -t -i:5000 -i:5001 -i:5002)
> ```

---

### Scenario 2: Only `python`, no `python3`

```sh
d=$(mktemp -d) \
  && printf '#!/bin/sh\necho "Python 3.11.0"\n' > $d/python \
  && printf '#!/bin/sh\nexit 127\n' > $d/python3 \
  && chmod +x $d/python $d/python3 \
  && PATH=$d:$PATH go run main.go install otel-python; rm -rf $d
```

**Expected:** works fine.

---

### Scenario 3: `venv` module missing  
Python 3 is present, but `python3 -m venv` fails.

```sh
d=$(mktemp -d) && printf '#!/bin/sh\ncase "$*" in\n  "--version") echo "Python 3.11.0" ;;\n  *) echo "No module named venv" >&2; exit 1 ;;\nesac\n' > $d/python3 && chmod +x $d/python3 && PATH=$d:$PATH go run main.go install otel-python; rm -rf $d
```

**Expected:**

```text
pip is not available for the detected Python 3 interpreter (/var/folders/rb/wh6hnhc16fddwwgw50bbh99r0000gq/T/tmp.TAdvb2xtEr/python3): exit status 1
    No module named venv
```

---

### Scenario 4: `pip` missing  
Python 3 is present, but `python3 -m pip` fails.

```sh
d=$(mktemp -d) && printf '#!/bin/sh\ncase "$*" in\n  "--version") echo "Python 3.11.0" ;;\n  *) echo "No module named pip" >&2; exit 1 ;;\nesac\n' > $d/python3 && chmod +x $d/python3 && PATH=$d:$PATH go run main.go install otel-python; rm -rf $d
```

**Expected:**

```text
pip is not available for the detected Python 3 interpreter (/var/folders/rb/wh6hnhc16fddwwgw50bbh99r0000gq/T/tmp.0NzWwPDyJ3/python3): exit status 1
    No module named pip
exit status 1
```

---

### Scenario 5: `venv` missing  
Python 3 and `pip` are present, but `python3 -m venv` fails.

```sh
d=$(mktemp -d) && printf '#!/bin/sh\ncase "$*" in\n  "--version") echo "Python 3.11.0" ;;\n  "-m pip"*) exit 0 ;;\n  "-m venv"*) echo "No module named venv" >&2; exit 1 ;;\nesac\n' > $d/python3 && chmod +x $d/python3 && PATH=$d:$PATH go run main.go install otel-python; rm -rf $d
```

**Expected:**

```text
venv module is not available for the detected Python 3 interpreter (/var/folders/rb/wh6hnhc16fddwwgw50bbh99r0000gq/T/tmp.YglRmrmHMI/python3) — on Debian/Ubuntu run: apt install python3-venv: exit status 1
    No module named venv
exit status 1
```


## Checklist

- [x] I have tested these changes locally.
- [x] I updated documentation where necessary.
- [x] I added or updated tests where appropriate.
- [x] I followed the existing code style and conventions.

Sourced from click's releases.

8.3.2

This is the Click 8.3.2 fix release, which fixes bugs but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes compared to the latest feature release.

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/click/8.3.2/ Changes: https://click.palletsprojects.com/page/changes/#version-8-3-2 Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/click/milestone/29

  • Fix handling of flag_value when is_flag=False to allow such options to be used without an explicit value. #3084 #3152
  • Hide Sentinel.UNSET values as None when using lookup_default(). #3136 #3199 #3202 #3209 #3212 #3224
  • Prevent _NamedTextIOWrapper from closing streams owned by StreamMixer. #824 #2991 #2993 #3110 #3139 #3140
  • Add comprehensive tests for CliRunner stream lifecycle, covering logging interaction, multi-threaded safety, and sequential invocation isolation. Add high-iteration stress tests behind a stress marker with a dedicated CI job. #3139
  • Fix callable flag_value being instantiated when used as a default via default=True. #3121 #3201 #3213 #3225

Updates `pyrefly` from 0.58.0 to 0.59.1
Release notes

Sourced from pyrefly's releases.

Pyrefly v0.59.1

Fixed a performance regression in 0.59.0.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly/compare/0.59.0...0.59.1

Pyrefly v0.59.0

Status : Beta
Release date: March 30, 2026

Pyrefly v0.59.0 bundles 153 commits from 20 contributors.

✨ New & Improved

Area What’s new
Type Checking - You can now use while...else statements with returns in the else clause without triggering a false positive missing-explicit-return error. - Pyrefly now correctly handles type inference for nested empty dictionaries when constructing TypedDict instances, avoiding implicit-any errors. - Error messages now highlight related code with inline labels; for example, an unsupported * operation will show the types of both operands directly in the source snippet
Language Server - LSP hover information for classes now displays constructor signature and docstring. - Support additional LSP functionality for notebooks, including find-references and rename.
Performance - Faster typechecking in large pythonc codebases, up to 2x faster on recent benchmarks on real world projects - Reduced CPU usage through smarter caching of module resolution results - Improved performance of the LSP server by reducing redundant workspace diagnostic publishes.

🐛 bug fixes

We closed 16 bug issues this release 👏

  • #2026: Fixed an issue where recursive bounded generics were incorrectly reported as object, ensuring accurate type checking.
  • #2812: Resolved a false positive invalid-type-var error when persisting the get method of a fully-annotated dict.
  • #2804: Fixed an implicit-any false positive that occurred with TypedDict items, improving code readability.
  • #2868: Pyrefly now correctly recognizes while...else statements with returns in the else clause as exhaustive.
  • #2814: Enhanced hover information for datetime.datetime imports to display constructor signatures and docstrings.
  • #2896: Fixed a bad-argument-type error that occurred when using double-underscore arguments.
  • #2893: Pyrefly now correctly handles dict Literal key types as subtypes of str key types.
  • #2865: Resolved an issue where tuple subclasses with overridden __getitem__ were not recognized.
  • #2871: Fixed a false positive error when using isinstance with type | X.
  • And more! #2444, #1270, #2900, #2862, #2853

Thank-you to all our contributors who found these bugs and reported them! Did you know this is one of the most helpful contributions you can make to an open-source project? If you find any bugs in Pyrefly we want to know about them! Please open a bug report issue here

📦 Upgrade

pip install --upgrade pyrefly==0.59.0

How to safely upgrade your codebase

... (truncated)

Updates `pytest` from 9.0.2 to 9.0.3
Release notes

Sourced from pytest's releases.

9.0.3

pytest 9.0.3 (2026-04-07)

Bug fixes

  • #12444: Fixed pytest.approx which now correctly takes into account ~collections.abc.Mapping keys order to compare them.

  • #13634: Blocking a conftest.py file using the -p no: option is now explicitly disallowed.

    Previously this resulted in an internal assertion failure during plugin loading.

    Pytest now raises a clear UsageError explaining that conftest files are not plugins and cannot be disabled via -p.

  • #13734: Fixed crash when a test raises an exceptiongroup with __tracebackhide__ = True.

  • #14195: Fixed an issue where non-string messages passed to unittest.TestCase.subTest() were not printed.

  • #14343: Fixed use of insecure temporary directory (CVE-2025-71176).

Improved documentation

  • #13388: Clarified documentation for -p vs PYTEST_PLUGINS plugin loading and fixed an incorrect -p example.
  • #13731: Clarified that capture fixtures (e.g. capsys and capfd) take precedence over the -s / --capture=no command-line options in Accessing captured output from a test function <accessing-captured-output>.
  • #14088: Clarified that the default pytest_collection hook sets session.items before it calls pytest_collection_finish, not after.
  • #14255: TOML integer log levels must be quoted: Updating reference documentation.

Contributor-facing changes

  • #12689: The test reports are now published to Codecov from GitHub Actions. The test statistics is visible on the web interface.

    -- by aleguy02

Updates the requirements on [click](https://github.com/pallets/click), [pyrefly](https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly), [pytest](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest), [ruff](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) and [setuptools](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools) to permit the latest version. Updates `click` from 8.3.1 to 8.3.2
Release notes

Sourced from click's releases.

8.3.2

This is the Click 8.3.2 fix release, which fixes bugs but does not otherwise change behavior and should not result in breaking changes compared to the latest feature release.

PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/click/8.3.2/ Changes: https://click.palletsprojects.com/page/changes/#version-8-3-2 Milestone: https://github.com/pallets/click/milestone/29

  • Fix handling of flag_value when is_flag=False to allow such options to be used without an explicit value. #3084 #3152
  • Hide Sentinel.UNSET values as None when using lookup_default(). #3136 #3199 #3202 #3209 #3212 #3224
  • Prevent _NamedTextIOWrapper from closing streams owned by StreamMixer. #824 #2991 #2993 #3110 #3139 #3140
  • Add comprehensive tests for CliRunner stream lifecycle, covering logging interaction, multi-threaded safety, and sequential invocation isolation. Add high-iteration stress tests behind a stress marker with a dedicated CI job. #3139
  • Fix callable flag_value being instantiated when used as a default via default=True. #3121 #3201 #3213 #3225
Changelog

Sourced from click's changelog.

Version 8.3.2

Released 2026-04-02

  • Fix handling of flag_value when is_flag=False to allow such options to be used without an explicit value. :issue:3084 :pr:3152
  • Hide Sentinel.UNSET values as None when using lookup_default(). :issue:3136 :pr:3199 :pr:3202 :pr:3209 :pr:3212 :pr:3224
  • Prevent _NamedTextIOWrapper from closing streams owned by StreamMixer. :issue:824 :issue:2991 :issue:2993 :issue:3110 :pr:3139 :pr:3140
  • Add comprehensive tests for CliRunner stream lifecycle, covering logging interaction, multi-threaded safety, and sequential invocation isolation. Add high-iteration stress tests behind a stress marker with a dedicated CI job. :pr:3139
  • Fix callable flag_value being instantiated when used as a default via default=True. :issue:3121 :pr:3201 :pr:3213 :pr:3225

Updates `pyrefly` from 0.58.0 to 0.59.1
Release notes

Sourced from pyrefly's releases.

Pyrefly v0.59.1

Fixed a performance regression in 0.59.0.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly/compare/0.59.0...0.59.1

Pyrefly v0.59.0

Status : Beta
Release date: March 30, 2026

Pyrefly v0.59.0 bundles 153 commits from 20 contributors.

✨ New & Improved

Area What’s new
Type Checking - You can now use while...else statements with returns in the else clause without triggering a false positive missing-explicit-return error. - Pyrefly now correctly handles type inference for nested empty dictionaries when constructing TypedDict instances, avoiding implicit-any errors. - Error messages now highlight related code with inline labels; for example, an unsupported * operation will show the types of both operands directly in the source snippet
Language Server - LSP hover information for classes now displays constructor signature and docstring. - Support additional LSP functionality for notebooks, including find-references and rename.
Performance - Faster typechecking in large pythonc codebases, up to 2x faster on recent benchmarks on real world projects - Reduced CPU usage through smarter caching of module resolution results - Improved performance of the LSP server by reducing redundant workspace diagnostic publishes.

🐛 bug fixes

We closed 16 bug issues this release 👏

  • #2026: Fixed an issue where recursive bounded generics were incorrectly reported as object, ensuring accurate type checking.
  • #2812: Resolved a false positive invalid-type-var error when persisting the get method of a fully-annotated dict.
  • #2804: Fixed an implicit-any false positive that occurred with TypedDict items, improving code readability.
  • #2868: Pyrefly now correctly recognizes while...else statements with returns in the else clause as exhaustive.
  • #2814: Enhanced hover information for datetime.datetime imports to display constructor signatures and docstrings.
  • #2896: Fixed a bad-argument-type error that occurred when using double-underscore arguments.
  • #2893: Pyrefly now correctly handles dict Literal key types as subtypes of str key types.
  • #2865: Resolved an issue where tuple subclasses with overridden __getitem__ were not recognized.
  • #2871: Fixed a false positive error when using isinstance with type | X.
  • And more! #2444, #1270, #2900, #2862, #2853

Thank-you to all our contributors who found these bugs and reported them! Did you know this is one of the most helpful contributions you can make to an open-source project? If you find any bugs in Pyrefly we want to know about them! Please open a bug report issue here

📦 Upgrade

pip install --upgrade pyrefly==0.59.0

How to safely upgrade your codebase

Updates `setuptools` to 82.0.1
Changelog

Sourced from setuptools's changelog.

v82.0.1

Bugfixes

  • Fix the loading of launcher manifest.xml file. (#5047)
  • Replaced deprecated json.__version__ with fixture in tests. (#5186)

Improved Documentation

  • Add advice about how to improve predictability when installing sdists. (#5168)

Misc

  • #4941, #5157, #5169, #5175

v82.0.0

Deprecations and Removals

  • pkg_resources has been removed from Setuptools. Most common uses of pkg_resources have been superseded by the importlib.resources <https://docs.python.org/3/library/importlib.resources.html>_ and importlib.metadata <https://docs.python.org/3/library/importlib.metadata.html>_ projects. Projects and environments relying on pkg_resources for namespace packages or other behavior should depend on older versions of setuptools. (#3085)

v81.0.0

Deprecations and Removals

  • Removed support for the --dry-run parameter to setup.py. This one feature by its nature threads through lots of core and ancillary functionality, adding complexity and friction. Removal of this parameter will help decouple the compiler functionality from distutils and thus the eventual full integration of distutils. These changes do affect some class and function signatures, so any derivative functionality may require some compatibility shims to support their expected interface. Please report any issues to the Setuptools project for investigation. (#4872)

v80.10.2

Bugfixes

  • Update vendored dependencies. (#5159)

Misc

title: python plugin update
body:
- linted docker plugin
- built-in runlike command in docker plugin (F3 on running docker process)
- new plugin uimgimage.py - use far2l image protocol for showing image from python plugin
- new plugin imagepdf.py - show pdf using image protocol
- change conflicting keys in uedreplace (fixes #3345)
- update info about open and install plugin from command line py:load <plugin> open

title: Modernise app from Python 2.7 to Python 3
body:
- New scryfall.py module replaces all dead external services (mtgimage.com,
  TCGPlayer scraper, MTGJson v2 URLs) with a single Scryfall API integration
  for card data, artwork, and prices
- Delete ConnectionRequired.py and UI/Prices.py (fully superseded)
- Port every file to Python 3: print(), .items(), str instead of basestring,
  explicit relative imports, tkinter.filedialog, integer division
- Fix Pillow deprecation: Image.ANTIALIAS → Image.LANCZOS
- Remove macOS-only osascript window-focus hack
- Replace distutils.version.StrictVersion (removed in 3.12) with no version
  check needed (Scryfall always current)
- Add background threading for image and price fetches so the UI never
  freezes; PIL→PhotoImage conversion kept on main thread to satisfy Tkinter
- Fix ScrollFrame mousewheel bug (self.scroll_frame.canvas → self.canvas)
  and raise-statement syntax (Python 2 → Python 3)
- Handle both MTGJson v2 (bare dict) and v5 ({"data":…} wrapper) formats
- Improve Collection price labels (Market/Foil/MTGO instead of High/Med/Low)
- Replace donothing() Help placeholder with a real About dialog

title: Rename package to arca-models
body:
## Summary

Renames all internal references from `data-archive-models` to `arca-models` to match the repo rename under the [Arca umbrella](https://github.com/eth-library/arca).

- Rename `schemas/data-archive/` → `schemas/arca/`
- Update Maven artifact, Java package (`ch.ethz.library.arca.model`), and GitHub Packages deploy URL in `pom.xml`
- Update `ci/mvn_settings.xml` repository URL
- Rename `src/data_archive/` → `src/arca_models/` and update `pyproject.toml`
- Regenerate `uv.lock`
- Update CI workflow name and paths
- Rename Java test package from `darc` to `arca`
- Update Python scripts and documentation

## Consumer migration

### Java

`pom.xml` — before:

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>ch.ethz.library</groupId>
    <artifactId>data-archive-models</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

After:

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>ch.ethz.library</groupId>
    <artifactId>arca-models</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

`.java` imports — before:

```java
import ch.ethz.library.darc.model.*;
```

After:

```java
import ch.ethz.library.arca.model.*;
```

### Python

`pyproject.toml` / `requirements.txt` — before:

```
data-archive-models
```

After:

```
arca-models
```

`.py` imports — before:

```python
from data_archive import ...
```

After:

```python
from arca_models import ...
```

The old `data-archive-models` packages on GitHub Packages and TestPyPI will be deleted after this lands.

title: Remove python backend
body:
The app goes from a client-server architecture (frontend + FastAPI backend on Azure) to a fully static site deployable on GitHub Pages, with the Python engine retained only for offline cache generation and CLI use.

## Details
- Removed the backend API — Deleted FastAPI server (api.py, models.py, server.py), Dockerfile, Azure
  deployment workflow, and all API tests (e2e, integration)
  - Renamed backend/ to engine/ — The Python package is now solely an offline computation engine and
  CLI, not a server. Updated pyproject.toml dependencies (dropped fastapi, uvicorn, httpx)
  - Made the frontend fully static — Added cache.js, positions.js, and tour.js modules so the UI
  computes everything client-side using precomputed JSON data instead of calling an API
  - Added static data pipeline — New generate_planets.py script and generate_test_fixtures.py to produce
   planets.json, windows.json, and golden test fixtures checked into frontend/data/ and
  frontend/test-fixtures/
  - Frontend tests — Added cache.test.js, positions.test.js, tour.test.js using golden fixtures from the
   Python engine
  - Updated CI/CD — ci.yml now runs both pytest and node --test; removed deploy-backend.yml; updated
  docs (CLAUDE.md, README.md)
  - Removed Azure infra scripts (infra/setup.sh, infra/map-credentials.sh)

title: Rewrite tooling in Java and drop Python scripts
body:
### Motivation
- Replace the existing Python-based tooling with Java implementations so the project is maintained in Java as requested (`refais tout en java / supprime le python`).
- Provide CLI programs that work with standard Java tooling and remove the Python dependency footprint.

### Description
- Added two Java CLI programs: `com.ratemywine.CollectRatings` (CSV-driven DuckDuckGo search + regex extraction) and `com.ratemywine.MilleniumWineRatingsScraper` (site crawler extracting JSON-LD and text ratings) under `src/main/java/com/ratemywine/`.
- Updated `README.md` with Java 17+ compilation (`javac -d out src/main/java/com/ratemywine/*.java`) and usage instructions for both tools.
- Removed the legacy Python scripts `millenium_wine_ratings_scraper.py` and `scripts/collect_ratings.py` and updated `requirements.txt` to indicate Python is no longer used.
- Added simple CLI parsing helpers and CSV/JSON output routines in the Java implementations to match previous output formats.

### Testing
- Compiled the Java sources with `javac -d out src/main/java/com/ratemywine/*.java` which succeeded.
- Ran the crawler with `java -cp out com.ratemywine.MilleniumWineRatingsScraper https://example.com --max-pages 1 --min-delay 0 --max-delay 0 --timeout 5 --csv /tmp/wine.csv --json /tmp/wine.json` and the program executed but failed HTTP requests due to environment network restrictions (`Network is unreachable`).
- Ran the CSV-driven flow with a sample file via `java -cp out com.ratemywine.CollectRatings --input /tmp/wines.csv --output /tmp/ratings.csv --max-results 1 --timeout 3 --sleep 0` which produced a valid output CSV containing a `no-candidate-page` status (expected given no network search results).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69a0bccd203883219fb6778344d7a9b1)

title: chore(deps-python): update python dependencies to 9.0.3 [SECURITY]
body:
This PR contains the following updates: | Package | Change | [Age](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [Confidence](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | |---|---|---|---| | [pytest](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest) ([changelog](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/changelog.html)) | `9.0.2` → `9.0.3` | ![age](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/age/pypi/pytest/9.0.3?slim=true) | ![confidence](https://developer.mend.io/api/mc/badges/confidence/pypi/pytest/9.0.2/9.0.3?slim=true) | ### GitHub Vulnerability Alerts #### [CVE-2025-71176](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-71176) pytest through 9.0.2 on UNIX relies on directories with the `/tmp/pytest-of-{user}` name pattern, which allows local users to cause a denial of service or possibly gain privileges. --- ### Release Notes
pytest-dev/pytest (pytest) ### [`v9.0.3`](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/releases/tag/9.0.3) [Compare Source](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/compare/9.0.2...9.0.3) ### pytest 9.0.3 (2026-04-07) #### Bug fixes - [#​12444](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/12444): Fixed `pytest.approx` which now correctly takes into account `~collections.abc.Mapping` keys order to compare them. - [#​13634](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13634): Blocking a `conftest.py` file using the `-p no:` option is now explicitly disallowed. Previously this resulted in an internal assertion failure during plugin loading. Pytest now raises a clear `UsageError` explaining that conftest files are not plugins and cannot be disabled via `-p`. - [#​13734](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13734): Fixed crash when a test raises an exceptiongroup with `__tracebackhide__ = True`. - [#​14195](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/14195): Fixed an issue where non-string messages passed to unittest.TestCase.subTest() were not printed. - [#​14343](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/14343): Fixed use of insecure temporary directory (CVE-2025-71176). #### Improved documentation - [#​13388](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13388): Clarified documentation for `-p` vs `PYTEST_PLUGINS` plugin loading and fixed an incorrect `-p` example. - [#​13731](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/13731): Clarified that capture fixtures (e.g. `capsys` and `capfd`) take precedence over the `-s` / `--capture=no` command-line options in `Accessing captured output from a test function `. - [#​14088](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/14088): Clarified that the default `pytest_collection` hook sets `session.items` before it calls `pytest_collection_finish`, not after. - [#​14255](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/14255): TOML integer log levels must be quoted: Updating reference documentation. #### Contributor-facing changes - [#​12689](https://redirect.github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/12689): The test reports are now published to Codecov from GitHub Actions. The test statistics is visible [on the web interface](https://app.codecov.io/gh/pytest-dev/pytest/tests). \-- by `aleguy02`

body: This allows us to build and test the extractor (for actual QL extraction -- not just the extractor unit tests) entirely from within the `github/codeql` repo, just as we do with Ruby. All that's needed is a `--search-path` argument that points to the repo root.

title: chore(wish/python): lower the Python version requirement
chore(python): keep only python in this repo
body:
Restructure from monorepo layout to standalone Python package repo.

- `rust/` — Rust extension with `pyroscope = "2.0.0"` from crates.io (not path dep)
- `python/` — Python package source (pyroscope + pyroscope_io)
- `scripts/tests/` — FFI compilation and integration test scripts
- `docker/` — wheel and musllinux Dockerfiles (adapted paths)
- `Makefile` — linux/musllinux/mac build targets, check/tag-version, ffi header/cffi generation
- Add `test_cargo_version_matches_python_version` Rust test (Cargo.toml vs pyproject.toml)
- Add `ci-rust.yml` workflow for Rust fmt/clippy/test
- Add tag-version verification step in release workflow
- Docker wheel build tested locally (manylinux2014 amd64)

Companion PR to remove Python from pyroscope-rs: https://github.com/grafana/pyroscope-rs/pull/488

title: Require Python >= 3.10 and support Python 3.14
body:
## What

Require Python >= 3.10 and support Python 3.14

## Why

Python 3.9 is End-of-Life and it's already not supported anymore by many libraries.

title: [python] Bump Python to 3.14.4
body:
## 0. Summary of Changes

**Release date:** April 7, 2026
**Scope:** ~337 bugfixes, build improvements, and documentation changes

## 1. Security

| CVE | Issue | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| CVE-2026-4224 | [gh-145986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145986) | Fix crash from unbounded C recursion in `xml.parsers.expat` when converting deeply nested XML content models with `ElementDeclHandler` |
| CVE-2026-3644 | [gh-145599](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145599) | Reject control characters in `http.cookies.Morsel.update()` and `BaseCookie.js_output()` |
| CVE-2026-2297 | [gh-145506](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145506) | Ensure `SourcelessFileLoader` uses `io.open_code` when opening `.pyc` files |
| — | [gh-144370](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144370) | Disallow control characters in status in `wsgiref.handlers` to prevent HTTP header injection |
| — | [gh-143930](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143930) | Reject leading dashes in URLs passed to `webbrowser.open()` |

## 2. Core and Builtins

### 2-1. Crash / Memory Safety Fixes

- **[gh-148157](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148157):** Fix crash when parsing invalid type comments for function parameters (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146615):** Fix crash in `__get__` for `METH_METHOD` descriptors with invalid second argument
- **[gh-145783](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145783):** Fix unlikely parser crash when errors were not propagated (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145792](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145792):** Fix out-of-bounds access invoking faulthandler without VLA support
- **[gh-145701](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145701):** Fix `SystemError` when `__classdict__` or `__conditional_annotations__` is in a class-scope inlined comprehension (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145335):** Fix crash in `os.pathconf` when called with `-1`
- **[gh-145234](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145234):** Fix `SystemError` in parser when encoding cookie decodes to carriage returns
- **[gh-145142](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145142):** Fix crash in free-threaded build when `str.maketrans` dict is concurrently modified
- **[gh-144872](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144872):** Fix heap buffer overflow in the parser (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-144766](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144766):** Fix crash in fork child process when perf support is enabled
- **[gh-144759](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144759):** Fix undefined behavior in lexer with `NULL` pointer arithmetic
- **[gh-144601](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144601):** Fix crash when importing a module whose `PyInit` function raises an exception from a subinterpreter
- **[gh-143636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143636):** Fix crash when calling `SimpleNamespace.__replace__()` on non-namespace instances
- **[gh-140594](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140594):** Fix out-of-bounds read when a single NUL character is read from stdin
- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** Fix `repr()` for lists and tuples containing `NULL`s

### 2-2. Free-Threaded Build Improvements

- **[gh-148144](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148144):** Initialize `_PyInterpreterFrame.visited` when copying frames so incremental GC doesn't read uninitialized bytes
- **[gh-146041](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146041):** Fix scaling bottleneck in `sys.intern` and `PyObject_SetAttr` by avoiding interpreter-wide lock
- **[gh-145779](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145779):** Improve scaling of `classmethod` and `staticmethod` calls
- **[gh-145685](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145685):** Improve scaling of type attribute lookups by avoiding contention on internal type lock
- **[gh-145713](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145713):** Make `bytearray.resize` thread-safe using critical sections
- **[gh-145615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145615):** Fix memory leak where mimalloc pages became permanently unreclaimable
- **[gh-145566](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145566):** Skip stop-the-world pause when reassigning `__class__` on newly created objects
- **[gh-145036](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145036):** Fix race condition in `list.__sizeof__`
- **[gh-144513](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144513):** Fix potential deadlock using critical sections during stop-the-world pauses
- **[gh-144446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144446):** Fix data races when reading frame object attributes while another thread executes the frame
- **[gh-144438](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144438):** Align QSBR thread state array to 64-byte cache line boundary to avoid false sharing
- **[gh-143650](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143650):** Fix race condition in `importlib` where a thread could receive a stale module reference

### 2-3. Other Core Fixes

- **[gh-146308](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146308):** Fix error handling issues in `_remote_debugging` module
- **[gh-146128](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146128):** Fix bug causing constant values to be partially corrupted in AArch64 JIT code
- **[gh-146250](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146250):** Fix memory leak in `SyntaxError` when re-initializing
- **[gh-146245](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146245):** Fix reference leaks in `socket` when audit hooks raise exceptions
- **[gh-146196](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146196):** Fix potential UB in `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteASCII` by adding zero-length check
- **[gh-146227](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146227):** Fix wrong type in `_Py_atomic_load_uint16` (C11 atomics backend)
- **[gh-146092](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146092):** Handle memory allocation failures on `str` and `float` opcodes
- **[gh-145990](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145990):** Sort `python --help-env` and `python --help-xoptions` output
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix GC tracking in `structseq.__replace__()` and reference leaks in error scenarios
- **[gh-142183](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142183):** Avoid pathological slowdown from repeated calls at a specific stack depth
- **[gh-145187](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145187):** Fix assertion fail when type parameter bound contains invalid expression in conditional block
- **[gh-144563](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144563):** Fix Tachyon profiler interaction with `ctypes` and modules loading Python shared library
- **[gh-130555](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130555):** Fix use-after-free in `dict.clear` with embedded values and re-entrant mutation
- **[gh-130327](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130327):** Fix erroneous clearing of `__dict__` if overwritten at runtime
- **[gh-141732](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141732):** Ensure `__repr__` for `ExceptionGroup` is not affected by subsequent mutation of original sequence
- **[gh-91636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/91636):** Clear weakrefs to unreachable objects created during GC finalizer execution
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** `\N{name}` escape syntax now supports CJK ideographs and Hangul syllables with case-insensitive names

## 3. Library

### 3-1. Regression Fix

- **[gh-144503](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144503):** Fix regression from 3.14.3 where `multiprocessing` forkserver would fail with `BrokenPipeError` when parent has very large `sys.argv`

### 3-2. Crash Fixes

- **[gh-146613](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146613):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` when grouper iterator is concurrently mutated
- **[gh-146080](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146080):** Fix `ssl` crash when SNI callback uses garbage-collected SSL object
- **[gh-146090](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146090):** Fix `sqlite3` crash when `create_collation` fails with `SQLITE_BUSY`; fix `MemoryError` vs `SystemError` in context callback allocation
- **[gh-145623](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145623):** Fix crash in `struct` calling `repr()` or `__sizeof__()` on uninitialized `Struct` object
- **[gh-145301](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145301):** Fix crashes in `hmac` and `hashlib` when C extension module initialization fails
- **[gh-144984](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144984):** Fix crash in `xml.parsers.expat.ExternalEntityParserCreate` on allocation failure
- **[gh-144833](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144833):** Fix use-after-free in `ssl` when `SSL_new()` returns `NULL`
- **[gh-143637](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143637):** Fix crash in `socket.sendmsg()` from re-entrant ancillary data mutation
- **[gh-143543](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143543):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` from re-entrant `__eq__` during key comparison
- **[gh-140652](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140652):** Fix crash in `_interpchannels.list_all` after closing a channel
- **[gh-142781](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142781):** Fix `zoneinfo.ZoneInfo` crash when internal class-level cache is inconsistent
- **[gh-142787](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142787):** Fix `sqlite3` assertion failure in blob subscript with empty slices

### 3-3. Security-Related Library Fixes

- **[gh-145883](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145883):** Fix heap buffer overflow reads from malformed TZif data in `zoneinfo` (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146310](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146310):** `ensurepip` no longer looks for `pip-*.whl` in the current directory
- **[gh-146083](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146083):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.5
- **[gh-144363](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144363):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.4

### 3-4. Free-Threaded Build Library Fixes

- **[gh-144777](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144777):** Fix data races in `io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder`
- **[gh-144809](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144809):** Make `collections.deque` copy atomic
- **[gh-144494](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144494):** Fix performance regression in `asyncio.all_tasks`
- **[gh-145446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145446):** Make `functools.partial` safer with keywords

### 3-5. Other Notable Library Fixes

- **[gh-146556](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146556):** Fix `annotationlib.get_annotations` hanging on circular `__wrapped__` chains
- **[gh-145633](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145633):** Fix `struct.pack('f', float)` to use `PyFloat_Pack4` and raise `OverflowError`
- **[gh-146076](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146076):** Fix `zoneinfo` crashes when deleting `_weak_cache` from a subclass
- **[gh-146054](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146054):** Limit size of `encodings.search_function` cache (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146004](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146004):** All `-X` options now propagated to child processes spawned by `multiprocessing`
- **[gh-145754](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145754):** Fix mock autospec with `FORWARDREF` annotation format
- **[gh-145750](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145750):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format string parsing (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145492](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145492):** Fix infinite recursion in `collections.defaultdict.__repr__` when self-referencing
- **[gh-145616](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145616):** Detect Android sysconfig ABI correctly on 32-bit ARM
- **[gh-145551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145551):** Fix `InvalidStateError` when cancelling `asyncio` subprocesses
- **[gh-145417](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145417):** Fix `venv` preserving incorrect SELinux context for `Activate.ps1`
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix double free and null pointer dereference in `hashlib` and `hmac`
- **[gh-145264](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145264):** Base64 decoder no longer ignores excess data after first padded quad (RFC 4648 conformance)
- **[gh-145158](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145158):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format strings
- **[gh-144986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144986):** Fix memory leak in `atexit.register()`
- **[gh-88091](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/88091):** Fix `unicodedata.decomposition` for Hangul characters
- **[gh-144782](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144782):** Fix `argparse.ArgumentParser` to be pickleable
- **[gh-144259](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144259):** Fix inconsistent display of long multiline pasted content in the REPL
- **[gh-144156](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144156):** Fix email header folding with RFC 2047 encoded words
- **[gh-66305](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/66305):** Fix hang on Windows in `tempfile` when directory is non-writable
- **[gh-140814](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140814):** `multiprocessing.freeze_support()` no longer sets default start method as side effect
- **[gh-144475](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144475):** Make `repr()` on `functools.partial` safer during concurrent attribute replacement
- **[gh-144538](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144538):** Bump bundled pip in `ensurepip` to 26.0.1
- **[gh-144316](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144316):** Fix crash in `_remote_debugging`
- **[gh-143698](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143698):** Allow `scheduler` and `setpgroup` to be `None` in `os.posix_spawn`; raise `TypeError` instead of `SystemError`
- **[gh-142516](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142516):** Fix `ssl.SSLContext` reference leaks
- **[gh-143304](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143304):** Fix `ctypes.CDLL` to honor the `handle` parameter on POSIX
- **[gh-142763](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142763):** Fix race condition between `ZoneInfo` creation and `clear_cache()`
- **[gh-142352](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142352):** Fix `asyncio.StreamWriter.start_tls` data loss when upgrading to TLS mid-stream
- **[gh-141707](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141707):** Don't change `TarInfo` type from `AREGTYPE` to `DIRTYPE` when parsing GNU long name headers
- **[gh-139933](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/139933):** Improve `AttributeError` suggestions for classes with custom `__dir__`
- **[gh-137335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137335):** Eliminate name conflicts for named pipes in `multiprocessing` and `asyncio` on Windows
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** Support lookup for Tangut Ideographs in `unicodedata`

## 4. Documentation

- **[gh-126676](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/126676):** Expand `argparse` documentation for `type=bool` with demonstration of surprising behavior
- **[gh-145649](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145649):** Fix text wrapping in `-X` option descriptions in the `python(1)` man page
- **[gh-145450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145450):** Document missing public `wave.Wave_write` getter methods
- **[gh-136246](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/136246):** New "Improve this page" link in docs sidebar

## 5. Build

- **[gh-146541](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146541):** Android testbed can now be built for 32-bit ARM and x86 targets
- **[gh-146498](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146498):** iOS XCframework build: ensure libpython isn't included in installed app content
- **[gh-146450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146450):** Android build script parity improvements
- **[gh-146446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146446):** iOS XCframework clean target is now more selective for single architectures
- **[gh-145801](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145801):** Use `-fprofile-update=atomic` with GCC PGO to prevent `.gcda` corruption

## 6. Windows

- **[gh-145307](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145307):** Defer loading of `psapi.dll` until used by `ctypes.util.dllist`
- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update bundled OpenSSL to 3.0.19
- **[gh-140131](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140131):** Fix REPL cursor position when module completion suggestion hits console width

## 7. macOS

- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update macOS installer to use OpenSSL 3.0.19
- **[gh-137586](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137586):** Invoke `osascript` with absolute path in `webbrowser` and `turtledemo`

## 8. C API

- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteRepr` now supports `NULL` argument
- **[gh-145010](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145010):** Use GCC dialect alternatives for inline assembly in `object.h` for `-masm=intel` compatibility
- **[gh-144981](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144981):** Make `PyUnstable_Code_SetExtra`, `PyUnstable_Code_GetExtra`, and `PyUnstable_Eval_RequestCodeExtraIndex` thread-safe on free-threaded builds

## 9. Tests

- **[gh-144418](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144418):** Android testbed emulator RAM increased from 2 GB to 4 GB
- **[gh-146202](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146202):** Fix race condition in regrtest temp directory creation on Windows
- **[gh-144739](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144739):** Skip `MemoryProtectionTest` when compiled with older expat but tests run with newer

*Source: [Python 3.14.4 Release](https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3144/) · [Full Changelog](https://docs.python.org/release/3.14.4/whatsnew/changelog.html)*
